tests.name: Online MCQ Test - 01
tests.instructionHtml:
Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Name the tax directly paid to the state? (The French Revolution)
questions.explanation: Taille was the tax paid directly to the state in France. This tax was for French peasantry and non-nobles in the Ancien Regime France. This tax was imposed on each household and was based on how much land was owned by peasants and non-nobles.

questions.question: When did Napoleon fight the Battle of Waterloo?
Who wrote the book 'The Social Contract'?
questions.explanation: Rousseau

questions.question: In how many estates French society was divided during the 18th century?
questions.explanation: Three

questions.question: Who was the king of France during the French Revolution?
questions.explanation: Louis XVI

questions.question: What was the immediate outcome of the storming of Bastille?

options.html: A meeting of the Estates General was called.
New constitution of France was framed.
French society was divided into I, II and III estate.
Fortress demolished and its stone fragments were sold in the markets as a souvenir.

questions.explanation: The Bastille was hated by all because it stood for the despotic power of the king. The fortress was demolished and its stone fragments were sold in the markets to all those who wished to keep a souvenir of its destruction.

questions.question: What was Directory?
questions.explanation: Two elected legislative councils would appoint an executive committee of five members, called Directory which would finally run the government.

questions.question: Reign of Terror ended with the end of which person?
questions.explanation: Robespierre was convicted by a court in July 1794, arrested and on the next day sent to the guillotine.

questions.question: Which ports owed their economic prosperity to the flourishing slave trade?
questions.explanation: Port cities like Bordeaux and Nantes owed their economic prosperity to the flourishing slave trade.

questions.question: Who participated in revolts against increasing taxes and food scarcity?
questions.explanation: In the past, peasants and workers had participated in revolts against increasing taxes and food scarcity. But they lacked the means and programmes to carry out full-scale measures that would bring about a change in the social and economic order.

questions.question: Give one word for: An extreme situation where the basic means of livelihood are endangered.
questions.explanation: Subsistence crisis is an extreme situation wheret basic means of livelihood are endangered.

questions.question: Which of the following is incorrect with respect to Directory?

options.html: The directory couldn't be dismissed by the vote of councils.
Two elected legislative councils would appoint an executive committee.
This system was introduced to check the concentration of power in one man.
Executive committee consist of two members.

questions.explanation: Executive committee consist of five members

questions.question: What was Sceptre?
questions.explanation: Sceptre was the symbol of royal power

questions.question: In India, who were inspired by the ideals for which the French fought?

options.html: Tipu Sultan and Ram Mohan Roy
B. R. Ambedkar and Bal Gangadhar Tilak

questions.explanation: In India, Tipu Sultan and Ram Mohan Roy were inspired by the ideals for which the French fought.

questions.question: Snake biting its tail to form a ring is a symbol of?
questions.explanation: Snake biting its tail to form a ring: Symbol of Eternity.

General Instructions:

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: The eye within a triangle radiating light stands for?
questions.explanation: The eye within a triangle radiating light: The all-seeing eye stands for knowledge.

questions.question: What is being signified by the political symbol The winged woman?
questions.explanation: The winged woman signifies Personification of the law

questions.question: The execution of Louis XVI and Marie Antoinette was done at which place?
questions.explanation: On 21 January, 1793 Louis XVI and his queen Marie Antoinette were executed publicly at the Place de la Concorde.

questions.question: Which book John Locke criticized the doctrine of the divine and absolute right of the monarch?
questions.explanation: In the book named Two Treatises of Government, John Locke criticized the doctrine of the divine and absolute right of the monarch.

questions.question: Who proposed a form of government based on a social contract between people and their representatives?
questions.explanation: Jean Jacques Rousseau proposed a form of government based on a social contract between people and their representatives in his book Social Contract.

questions.question: Which of the following law was not made by Robespierre?

options.html: Peasants were to take their grains to the cities to sell.
Laws were made to fix the maximum wages and prices.
The prices were fixed by the Monarch.

questions.explanation: The prices were fixed by the government.

questions.question: Castle or stately residence belonging to a king or a nobleman is called as?
questions.explanation: Chateau - Castle or stately residence belonging to a king or a nobleman.

questions.question: Name an influential pamphlet written by Abbe Sieyes.
questions.explanation: Abbe Sieyes, originally a priest, wrote an influential pamphlet called What is the Third Estate?

questions.question: While drafting the constitution of 1791, what was the main objective of the National Assembly in France?

options.html: To give right of freedom to women
To limit the powers of Monarch
To give equal right to vote

questions.explanation: The main objective of National Assembly in France was to limit the powers of Monarch.

questions.question: Who suggested the division of power within the government between the legislative, the executive and the judiciary?
questions.explanation: Montesquieu

questions.question: The description relating to the division of power within the government between the legislative, the executive and the judiciary is mentioned in which book?
questions.explanation: In The Spirit of the Laws, Montesquieu proposed a division of power within the government between the legislative, the executive and the judiciary.

questions.question: Which of the following option is true with respect to the qualification of Elector?

options.html: A man has paid tithe amounting to One-tenth of the agricultural produce.
A man had to belong to the higher bracket of taxpayer.
A man has paid taxes equal to at least 3 days of a labourer’s wage.
A man is above the age of 25 years.

questions.explanation: To qualify as an elector and as a member of the Assembly, a man had to belong to the higher bracket of taxpayer.

questions.question: The most revolutionary social reforms of the Jacobin regime was?

options.html: The abolition of slavery in the French colonies

questions.explanation: One of the most revolutionary social reforms of the Jacobin regime was the abolition of slavery in the French colonies.

questions.question: The political instability of the Directory paved the way for the rise of which military dictator?
questions.explanation: The political instability of the Directory paved the way for the rise of a military dictator, Napoleon Bonaparte.

questions.question: The status of active citizens was given to whom?

options.html: Only the man above 25 years paying taxes equal to at least 3 days of a labourer’s wage
Only the members of National Assembly
Only a man belonging to the higher bracket of taxpayer
Only the man who had the property

questions.explanation: Only men above 25 years of age who paid taxes equal to at least 3 days of a labourer’s wage were given the status of active citizens.

Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Strength lies in unity is portrayed by which political symbols?

options.html: The bundle of rods or fasces

questions.explanation: The bundle of rods or fasces: One rod can be easily broken, but not an entire bundle. Strength lies in unity.

questions.question: Who got the right to vote in the France Constitution of 1791?
questions.explanation: Not all citizens had the right to vote. Only men above 25 years of age who paid taxes equal to at least 3 days of a labourer’s wage were given the status of active citizens, that is, they were entitled to vote.

questions.question: Which of the following option is incorrect with respect to France Constitution, 1791?

options.html: It began with a declaration of the Rights of Man and Citizen
There was the mention of active and passive citizens
Every citizen were given equal right to vote
It vested the power to make laws in the National Assembly

questions.explanation: Not all citizens had the right to vote. Only men above 25 years of age who paid taxes equal to at least 3 days of a labourer’s wage were given the status of active citizens, that is, they were entitled to vote.

questions.question: The duty to protect citizen’s natural rights was vested to whom?
questions.explanation: It was the duty of the state to protect each citizen’s natural rights.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a natural and inalienable right in the Constitution of France?
questions.explanation: The Constitution began with a Declaration of the Rights of Man and Citizen. Rights such as the right to life, freedom of speech, freedom of opinion, equality before law, were established as natural and inalienable rights, that is, they belonged to each human being by birth and could not be taken away.

questions.question: The speech of Robespierre was printed in which newspaper?
questions.explanation: On 7 February 1794, Robespierre made a speech at the Convention, which was then carried by the newspaper Le Moniteur Universel.

questions.question: Which of the following was not the reason of subsistence crisis?

options.html: Worker’s wages did not keep pace with the rise in prices
Drought or hail reduced the harvest
The rapid increase in the demand for food grains

questions.explanation: Occurrence of a great famine was not the reason of subsistence crisis

questions.question: Which group of persons invested with special functions in the church?
questions.explanation: Clergy were the group of persons invested with special functions in the church.

questions.question: Which of the following option is incorrect with respect to 3rd Estate?

options.html: They enjoyed certain privileges by birth
The burden of financing activities of the state through taxes was borne by the third estate.
They were obliged to render services in the army or to participate in building roads.
All members of the third estate had to pay taxes to the state.

questions.explanation: The members of the first two estates, that is, the clergy and the nobility, enjoyed certain privileges by birth.

questions.question: How much amount of tithe was levied from peasants by the church?

options.html: One-tenth of the income of family members

questions.explanation: The Church extracted its share of taxes called tithes from the peasants , comprising one-tenth of the agricultural produce.

questions.question: Taille was to be paid directly to?
questions.explanation: Taille was a kind of tax to be paid directly to the state

questions.question: Who was one of the most important of the politically active women in revolutionary France?
questions.explanation: Olympe de Gouges was one of the most important of the politically active women in revolutionary France. She protested against the Constitution and the Declaration of Rights of Man and Citizen as they excluded women from basic rights that each human being was entitled to

questions.question: Which group were obliged to render service to in the army or to participate in building roads?
questions.explanation: Peasants were obliged to render services to the lord - to work in his house and fields - to serve in the army or to participate in building roads

questions.question: Which of the following work were peasants obliged to perform?

options.html: To work in lord’s house and field

questions.explanation: Peasants were obliged to render services to the lord - to work in his house and fields - to serve in the army or to participate in building roads

questions.question: Which group was exempted from paying feudal dues?
questions.explanation: The nobles enjoyed feudal privileges. These included feudal dues, which they extracted from the peasants.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Which group enjoyed certain privileges by birth?
questions.explanation: The members of the first two estates, that is, the clergy and the nobility, enjoyed certain privileges by birth.

questions.question: What was the unit of currency in France, that discontinued in 1794?
questions.explanation: Livre was the unit of currency in France, discontinued in 1794

questions.question: Which of the following group comprised the Third Estate comprised?
questions.explanation: The Third Estate comprised poor servants, small peasants, landless labourers, peasants artisans, big businessmen, merchants and lawyers.

questions.question: 2nd estate comprised of which group?
questions.explanation: 2nd estate comprised of Nobility

questions.question: 1st estate comprised of which group?
questions.explanation: 1st estate comprised of Clergy

questions.question: Which of the following is not the reason of empty treasure?

options.html: The cost of maintaining an extravagant court.
Food supplies to the ordinary citizens.

questions.explanation: Long years of war had drained the financial resources of France. Added to this was the cost of maintaining an extravagant court at the immense palace of Versailles. To meet its regular expenses, such as the cost of maintaining an army, the court, running government offices or universities, the state was forced to increase taxes.

questions.question: Whom did Louis XVI get married?
questions.explanation: In 1774, Louis XVI of the Bourbon family of kings ascended the throne of France. He was 20 years old and married to the Austrian princess Marie Antoinette.

questions.question: What is the Old Regime?

options.html: The society and institutions of France before 1789
The society and institutions of France after 1789
The period when the storming of the Bastille occurred
The period when the French society divided into a different estate.

questions.explanation: The term Old Regime is usually used to describe the society and institutions of France before 1789.

questions.question: The storming of the Bastille occurred on the morning of?
questions.explanation: On the morning of 14 July 1789, the city of Paris was in a state of alarm. The king had commanded troops to move into the city. Rumors spread that he would soon order the army to open fire upon the citizens. Some 7,000 men and women gathered in front of the town hall and decided to form a people’s militia. They broke into a number of government buildings in search of arms.

questions.question: Why Bastille was hated by people?

options.html: It stood for the despotic power of the citizens.
It stood for the despotic power of the military.
It stood for the despotic power of the king.
It stood for the despotic power of the clergy.

questions.explanation: Bastille was hated by all, because it stood for the despotic power of the king. The fortress was demolished and its stone fragments were sold in the markets to all those who wished to keep a souvenir of its destruction.

questions.question: Napoleon Bonaparte was defeated in:
questions.explanation: Napoleon Bonaparte was finally defeated at Waterloo in 1815.

questions.question: Directory means:

options.html: An executive made up of five members

questions.explanation: A new constitution was introduced which denied the vote to non-propertied sections of society. It provided for two elected legislative councils. These then appointed a Directory, an executive made up of five members.

questions.question: Marseilles is a:
questions.explanation: Marseilles, composed by the poet Roget de L’Isle. It was sung for the first time by volunteers from Marseilles as they marched into Paris and so got its name. The Marseillaise is now the national anthem of France.

questions.question: Passive citizens of France were:

options.html: Men and women who didn't vote

questions.explanation: Only men above 25 years of age who paid taxes equal to at least 3 days of a labourer’s wage were given the status of active citizens, that is, they were entitled to vote. The remaining men and all women were classed as passive citizens.

questions.question: Two Treatises of Government was written by:
questions.explanation: Two Treatises of Government was written by John Locke. In this, he has sought to refute the doctrine of the divine and absolute right monarch.

chapters.name: HIS Socialism in Europe and the Russian Revolution

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Which Revolution of Russia is related to Bloody Sunday?
Who was the emperor of Russia at the start of the First World War?
questions.explanation: Tsar Nicholas II

questions.question: Who propounded the idea of a communist society?
questions.explanation: Karl Marx

questions.question: Who in France, wanted the government to encourage cooperatives and replace capitalist enterprises?
questions.explanation: Louis Blanc

questions.question: Who sought to build a cooperative community called New Harmony?
questions.explanation: Robert Owen

questions.question: Who put forward the concept of April theses?
questions.explanation: Lenin put forward the concept of April theses under which banks were nationalised, lands were given to the peasants and war was put to an end.

questions.question: Which organization did socialists form to spread the socialist ideas?
questions.explanation: Workers and socialists in countries like Germany and England formed the Second International association talking for their rights.

questions.question: What was the secret police of Russia called?
questions.explanation: Cheka was the secret police of Russia which was later called OGPU and NKVD.

questions.question: Under whom the military committee called the Red Guards formed?
questions.explanation: Trotsky formed this committee soon after the October Revolution.

questions.question: Who nationalised the banks and industries in Russia?
questions.explanation: Lenin nationalised the industries and banks soon after the October Revolution.

questions.question: Who is known as the Father of Communism?
questions.explanation: Karl Marx a German philosopher is regarded as the Father of Communism. He fled from Germany to live in England as he was considered a political threat in Germany.

questions.question: Who led the procession of workers in St Petersburg in 1905?
questions.explanation: Father Georgi Gapon led the peaceful procession and was fired upon by the Russian troops when they reached near the Winter Palace killing 100 and injuring about 300 people.

questions.question: How many people practised agriculture in Russia before the Revolution?
questions.explanation: 85% of people did farming in Russia which was very high as compared to other European nations.

questions.question: Name the Muslim reformers within the Russian empire.
questions.explanation: Jadidist were the Muslim modernist reformers within the Russian empire. They normally called themselves as progressives and intellectuals.

questions.question: What was Comintern?
questions.explanation: Comintern or the Communist international was an organisation set up by Lenin to sponsor communist revolutions all over the world.

questions.question: Lenin made a peace treaty with which country after World War I?
questions.explanation: After much opposition Lenin made peace treaty with Germany in 1918.

questions.question: Karl Marx was against which society?
questions.explanation: Karl Marx thought that the Serfs exploit the peasants so he was against the accumulation of wealth by few people in the society.

questions.question: When is World Labour Day celebrated?
questions.explanation: International Worker's Day or World Labour Day is also known as May Day is celebrated on 1 May every year.

questions.question: What was the capital of Russia till 1917?
questions.explanation: St. Petersburg was the capital of Russia before Moscow which was renamed as Petrograd later on.

questions.question: What was the collective farm called in Russia?
questions.explanation: Kolkhoz is a Russian name for collective farms.

questions.question: Name the Committee organized by Leon Trotskii during February Revolution.
questions.explanation: Trotsky organized this committee and was called the Commissar of the war in the new Soviet government.

questions.question: Who was the military commander during Lenin's February Revolution?
questions.explanation: He wad the founder and commander of the Red army during February Revolution.

questions.question: Bolshevik party was later renamed as?
questions.explanation: In 1918 the bolshevik party changed its name to Russian Communist Party at Lenin's suggestion.

questions.question: Who was called whites during the Russian Civil war?
questions.explanation: The anti-bolsheviks or pro-tsarists were called as the 'whites'.

questions.question: Who were called greens during the civil war in Russia?
questions.explanation: Greens were the armed peasant socialist revolutionaries who fought against all governments in Russian civil war from 1917 to 1922.

questions.question: What slogan was shouted during the February revolution?
questions.explanation: As there was acute shortage of food and peace in the country.

questions.question: Rasputin was a monk who made________ unpopular.
questions.explanation: During his period the condition of peasants and workers became miserable and the defeat of Russia in World War-I angered people.

questions.question: The name of St Petersburg was changed to
questions.explanation: Petersburg sounded too German name so it was changed to Petrograd.

questions.question: Bolsheviks were also called as?
questions.explanation: Bolsheviks were the ones who fought as revolutionaries in the Russian Revolution.

questions.question: 8th March is celebrated as which day?
questions.explanation: Women's day is celebrated on 8th March to help nations to eliminate discrimination against women.

questions.question: The river in Petrograd was named as?
questions.explanation: Neva is a river that flows in north western part of Russia where Petrograd city is situated.

questions.question: In which year was USSR formed?
questions.explanation: The USSR was formed in 1922 which was called Union of Soviet Socialist Republic.

questions.question: Name the vessel used in October Revolution.
questions.explanation: Aurora was one of the three vessels used by the Russians during the Russian Revolution.

questions.question: Who started the new economic policy in Russia?
questions.explanation: Lenin instigated the new economic policy also known as 'April Theses'.

questions.question: Which demands of the following were not included in April Theses of Lenin?
questions.explanation: The demand of formation of Duma was not included in 'April Theses' by Lenin.

questions.question: What does the word Soviet mean?
questions.explanation: Soviet in Russian language means a council or an organisation of workers.

questions.question: Which among the following countries was a member of the Allies?
questions.explanation: Russia was one of the active members of 'Allies' whereas the other three countries were the members of 'Axis powers'.

questions.question: Who headed the party after the death of Lenin?
questions.explanation: After Lenin's death Stalin became the head of the Bolshevik party.

questions.question: What was kulak?
questions.explanation: Kulaks were the wealthy peasants of Russia.

questions.question: Which of the following refers to women's right to vote?
questions.explanation: Suffragette was a movement to give voting rights to women.

questions.question: What was the Russian Parliament called?
questions.explanation: The Russian elected Parliament was called Duma which made laws for the country.

questions.question: What slogan was shouted in Russia during the February Revolution?
questions.explanation: They wanted to see all the people as equal so they shouted for food and peace for everyone.

questions.question: Which calender did Russia use until February 1918?
questions.explanation: The Russians used the Gregorian calender until 1918 after which they started using the Soviet Eternal calender.

questions.question: During which international event did the Russian Revolution take place?
questions.explanation: Russian revolution took place in the year 1917 i.e. during the first world war.

questions.question: What was the name of the Russian Parliament?
questions.explanation: Duma was the elected consultative Parliament of Russia.

tests.name: Online MCQ Test - 04
tests.instructionHtml:
Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Who were kulaks?
questions.explanation: Kulaks were well-to-do farmers of Russia who were forced to do collective farming during the period of Stalin.

questions.question: When was Comintern formed?
questions.explanation: It was the international union of pro-Bolshevik socialist parties formed in 1919 after the Russian Revolution.

questions.question: What was budeonovka?
questions.explanation: It was the part of new uniform that was designed for the army and officials.

questions.question: What was the other name of the October Revolution?
questions.explanation: Because it was carried by the Bolsheviks who were also called the 'Reds'.

questions.question: Who was the writer of the books The Communist Manifesto and Das Capital?
questions.explanation: Karl Marx was a revolutionary who initiated the ideas of communism and wrote many books on it.

questions.question: Who was the leader of the Bolsheviks?
questions.explanation: Lenin wanted to unite all the people of Russia and so was elected as the leader if the Bolsheviks.

questions.question: Who was the king of Russia in 1914?
questions.explanation: Tsar Nicholas -II was crowned as the king in 1894. He was abdicated in 1917.

questions.question: Which group opened their minds to the need for change?
questions.explanation: The conservatives opposed radicals and the liberals but started accepting some changes after the Russian Revolution.

questions.question: Which group opposed privileges enjoyed by landowners and wealthy society?
questions.explanation: The conservatives believed in equal distribution of wealth.

questions.question: Which group did not believe in Universal Adult Franchise in Russia?
questions.explanation: The liberals did not believe in giving voting rights to women.

questions.question: A special secret police was formed by Bolsheviks called:
questions.explanation: The secret police (called the Cheka first, and later OGPU and NKVD) punished those who criticised the Bolsheviks.

questions.question: The Jadidists were those who:
questions.explanation: Jadidists were the Muslim reformers within the Russian empire.

questions.question: The Central Powers of World War I were:
questions.explanation: In 1914, war broke out between two European alliances – Germany, Austria, and Turkey (the Central Powers) and France, Britain, and Russia (later Italy and Romania).

questions.question: The event, 'Bloody Sunday' was later on called:
questions.explanation: The incident, known as Bloody Sunday, started a series of events that became known as the 1905 Revolution.

questions.question: Louis Blanc in France believed in:

options.html: A government to encourage cooperatives and replace capitalists

questions.explanation: In France Louis Blanc (1813-1882) wanted the government to encourage cooperatives and replace capitalist enterprises.

chapters.name: HIS Nazism and the Rise of Hitler

tests.name: Online MCQ Test - 01
tests.instructionHtml:
Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: What is the name of the German Parliament?
questions.explanation: Reichstag is a German word which means parliament.

questions.question: What was the name of secret state police during the Nazi regime?
questions.explanation: Gestapo

questions.question: Which Republic was born in Germany after the First World War?
questions.explanation: Weimar

questions.question: Which of these countries was related to Allied powers:
questions.explanation: The principal members of the Allies were the United Kingdom, the Soviet Union, the United States, and China (the “Big Four”)

questions.question: The German Parliament is known as
questions.explanation: During World War 1 the Reichstag - (Diet of the Realm or Imperial Diet) was the Parliament of Germany from 1871 to 1918. Legislation was shared between the Reichstag and the Bundesrat, which was the Imperial Council of the reigning princes of the German States.

questions.question: Which of the following was not a feature of the new Nazi-style of politics?

options.html: Not so powerful speeches by Hitler

questions.explanation: In the new style of nazi politics, there were no ferocious and strong speeches by Hitler.

questions.question: Which of the following was not a part of Hitler’s policies to exclude Jews?
questions.explanation: Assimilation was not part of Hitler's policy to exclude Jews.

questions.question: Which was not a feature of Jew stereotypes?
questions.explanation: Jews were considered as the killer of Christ and usurers. They were considered undesirables and were selfish and uncharitable.

questions.question: In which place was the International War Tribunal setup?
questions.explanation: At the end of World War 2, the international military tribunal was set up at Nuremberg to prosecute Nazi war criminals for crimes against war, crimes against peace and humanity.

questions.question: Which was the worst concentration camp during the Second World War?
questions.explanation: Genocidal War – under the shadow of 2nd world war Germany mass murdered 6 million Jews, 200,000 Gypsies, 1 million Polish civilians, 70,000 Germans who were considered mentally and physically disabled – by gassing them in killing centres like Auschwitz.

questions.question: World War Second began with the German invasion of
questions.explanation: Germany invades Poland in the year 1939, German forces bombard Poland on land and from the air, as Adolf Hitler sought to regain lost territory and ultimately rule Poland. World War II had begun. The German invasion of Poland was a primary reason for the onset of the 2nd World War.

questions.question: Who were the Desirables?
questions.explanation: According to Nazi ideology, there was no equality among humans but only racial hierarchy. As per Hitler's racism borrowed from Charles Darwin, the Nordic German Aryans were considered pure blood and hence considered as desirables.

questions.question: Who among the following topped the list of undesirables?
questions.explanation: Jews were stereotyped as the killer of Christ and Usurers and hence they were considered as Undesirables.

questions.question: What was not a factor in the rise of Hitler?

options.html: Years of Depression and Economic crisis
Nazi propoganda and Hitler’s charismatic leadership

questions.explanation: After losing in the 1st World war German National assembly met at Weimar and established a democratic constitution with federal structures. Deputies were elected to German Parliament or Reichstag – based on an equal vote by all adults including women. But it couldn’t remain long and the Nazi Party headed by Hitler took over. Hence Weimar did no play a major role in the rise of Hitler's power.

questions.question: During the Second World War which country waged a Genocidal War?
questions.explanation: Under the shadow of 2nd world war Germany waged a Genocidal War–mass murder killing 6 million Jews, 200,000 Gypsies, 1million Polish civilians, 70,000 Germans who were considered mentally and physically disabled by gassing them in killing centres like Auschwitz.

tests.instructionHtml:

questions.question: When was Adolf Hitler born?
questions.explanation: Adolf Hitler was born in the year 1889.

questions.question: Where was Adolf Hitler born?
questions.explanation: Adolf Hitler was born in Austria.

questions.question: Which act established the dictatorship in Germany?
questions.explanation: In the year 1933, the famous Enabling Act was passed which established Dictatorship in Germany.

questions.question: When did Hitler reoccupy Rheinland?
questions.explanation: Hitler reoccupied Rheinland in the year 1936.

questions.question: One people One ________ and One Leader
questions.explanation: Hitler integrated Austria and Germany under the slogan One people one empire and one leader.

questions.question: When was the Tripartite Pact signed?
questions.explanation: The Tripartite Pact was signed between Germany, Italy and Japan in September 1940.

questions.question: When did Germany invade Poland?
questions.explanation: Germany invaded Poland in September 1939, this started a war with France and England.

questions.question: In which place did Soviet army defeat Germany?
questions.explanation: When Germany attacked Soviet Union in 1941, it was defeated at Stalingrad by the Soviet Army.

questions.question: The US dropped an atom bomb on ________ in Japan.
questions.explanation: After World War 2 ended on May 1945, the US dropped an atom bomb on Hiroshima to avenge the bombing on Pearl Harbour.

questions.question: When did World War Second end?
questions.explanation: World War Second ended on May 1945.

questions.question: Which of the following was the immediate factor of the Great Depression (1929-1932)?

options.html: Collapse of the Wall Street Exchange

questions.explanation: In the year 1929, the Wall Street Exchange collapsed. 13 million shares were sold in a single day, which led to great economic depression.

questions.question: Hitler's ideas of racialism were based on which of the following thinkers
questions.explanation: Hitler's idea of racialism was borrowed from Charles Darwin-evolution and natural selection. In this idea, Nordic German Aryans were at the top and Jews at the lowest rank.

questions.question: What was Jungvolk?

options.html: Nazi Youth group for children above the age of 14.
Nazi Youth group for children below 14 years.

questions.explanation: The youth group for children below the age of 14 was known as Jungvolk.

questions.question: Which of the following was the State Secret Police?
questions.explanation: The Gestapo were the secret state police and were the strongest and most feared.

questions.question: Hitler's world view was based on the principals of:

options.html: One nation, One Empire, One Leader

questions.explanation: Lebensraum means living space wherein new territories were to be acquired for settlement. He believed in acquiring vast territories to spread the race of Nordic German Aryans.

General Instructions:

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Which of the following was not true of Nazi State and Women?

options.html: Equal rights for men and women.
All women were not treated equally.
Women were the bearer of the Pure blooded Aryan Children.

questions.explanation: There were no equal rights for men and women. Children were told that men were different from women and hence the fight for equal rights would destroy the society.

questions.question: Which of the following is not true about the ordinary people in Germany?

options.html: Many believed that Nazi Government would bring prosperity.
They were scared to act, to protest, to differ.

questions.explanation: Not every German was a Nazi, there were many who did not support the Nazi ideologies and principals.

questions.question: What was the Nazi killing operation called?
questions.explanation: The sufferings endured by Jews during the Nazi killing operation was called the Holocaust.

questions.question: Reichstag refers to
questions.explanation: German parliament is called Reichstag.

questions.question: Who were the November Criminals?
questions.explanation: Socialists, Catholics, and Democrats who supported the Weimar Republic after the war became easy targets of attack in the conservative nationalist circles and were called November Criminals.

questions.question: Who from the following were not November Criminals?
questions.explanation: The revolutionary Spartacists did not support the Weimar Republic, they came together and founded the Communist Party of Germany.

questions.question: What is Dawes Plan?
questions.explanation: Germany faced Hyperinflation because of the reckless printing of paper currency which led to a tremendous increase in price. The US intervened with the Dawes Plan to ease German of its financial burden.

questions.question: What was the name given to the gas chamber by Nazi?
questions.explanation: Gas chambers were known as disinfection-areas and looked like bathrooms equipped with fake showerheads.

questions.question: The Great Depression was a period of
questions.explanation: Wall Street Exchange collapsed in one day, around 13 million shares were sold – the start of economic depression. The national income of the USA halved. Factories shut down, farmers were hit, export fell and speculators took money off the market. The industrial production of Germany reduced to 40%. People lost jobs, paid reduced wages, unemployment at 6 million. Criminal activities increased. Savings of old age lost as current lost its value. Peasants couldn’t fill the stomach. All these factors indicate that the Great Depression was an Economic Crisis.

questions.question: The US entered the 2nd World War due to
questions.explanation: When Japan extended its support to Germany and bombed the US base at Pearl Harbor, US entered the 2nd World War.

questions.question: Who was assigned the responsibility of economic recovery by Hitler?
questions.explanation: Hitler assigned the responsibility of economic recovery to economist Hjalmar Schacht who aimed at full production and full employment through state-funded work creation program.

questions.question: When did Hitler join German Workers Party?
questions.explanation: Hitler joined The German Workers Party in 1919.

questions.question: Which party did Hitler join in 1919?
questions.explanation: In 1919 Hitler joined the German Workers Party

questions.question: The Nazi party became the largest party by
questions.explanation: In 1932 the Nazi party got more than 37% votes, thus making it the largest party.

questions.question: When did Hitler try to seize control of Bavaria and capture Berlin?
questions.explanation: Hitler planned to seize control of Bavaria, marched to Berlin and capture power in 1923 but failed.

Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: What is The Fire Decree?

options.html: Suspension of Civic rights like Freedom of speech, press and assembly
Depriving people of food and clothing

questions.explanation: After becoming the Chancellor, he suspended the Civic rights like Freedom of speech, press, and assembly through The Fire Decree.

questions.question: Who offered Chancellorship to Hitler?
questions.explanation: In 1932 the Nazi party became the largest party with more than 37% votes. So in 1933, President Hindenburg offered the Chancellorship to Hitler, the highest position amongst the cabinet of ministers.

questions.question: When did Hitler became Chancellor of Germany?
questions.explanation: During the great economic depression, Nazism became a mass movement. By 1932 Hitler's party became the largest party with 37% votes. By 30 January 1933 Hitler became the Chancellor the highest position in the cabinet of ministers.

questions.question: Which Treaty ended World War 1?
questions.explanation: The Treaty of Versailles was the most important of the peace treaties that brought World War 1 to an end. The Treaty ended the state of war between Germany and the Allied Powers. Germany was demilitarized and had to pay a huge amount of compensation.

questions.question: When was the First World War fought?
questions.explanation: The First World War was fought between the Central Powers (Germany, Austria, and Turkey) and Allied Powers (France, Britain, and Russia) between 1914-1918.

questions.question: Which German minister committed suicide in Berlin in April?
questions.explanation: Joseph Goebbels was the Propaganda Minister in Hitler's Government, who committed suicide alongside Hitler in the month of April. He said that he felt compelled to remain with Hitler "for reasons of humanity and personal loyalty".

questions.question: Which are the allied powers?
questions.explanation: The allied powers were led by UK, France, USSR, USA. Initially, it was led by the UK and France. In 1941 they were joined by USSR and the USA. They fought against the axis powers, namely Germany, Italy and Japan.

questions.question: In which year did Germany surrender to the Allies?
questions.explanation: In May 1945 German surrendered to the allies. The allied power was led by UK France and USSR.

questions.question:
Arrange the following in correct sequence with respect to the period in which the event occurred:

  1. Hitler attacked the Soviet Union
  2. Hitler reoccupied the Rhineland
  3. Hitler integrated Austria and Germany
  4. The French occupied Ruhr, to claim their coal.
questions.explanation:
iv. In 1923, the French occupied Ruhr, to claim their coal.
ii. Hitler reoccupied the Rhineland in 1936.
iii. Hitler integrated Austria and Germany in 1938.
i. Hitler attacked the Soviet Union in June 1941.

questions.question:
Arrange the following in correct sequence with respect to the period in which the event occurred:

  1. Nazi Party became the largest party with 37 percent votes.
  2. Hitler planned to seize control of Bavaria.
  3. Adolf Hitler born in Austria.
  4. The Nazi Party got not more than 2. 6 percent votes in the Reichstag.
questions.explanation:
iii. In 1889, Adolf Hitler born in Austria.
ii. In 1923, Hitler planned to seize control of Bavaria.
iv. In 1928, the Nazi Party got not more than 2. 6 percent votes in the Reichstag.
i. By 1932, Nazi Party became the largest party with 37 percent votes.

questions.question:
Arrange the following in correct sequence with respect to the period in which the event occurred:

  1. Wall Street Exchange crashed.
  2. Political radicalisation was heightened by the economic crisis.
  3. Industrial production was reduced to 40 percent.
  4. Hitler joined a small group called the German Workers’ Party.
questions.explanation:
iv. In 1919, Hitler joined a small group called the German Workers’ Party.
ii. Political radicalisation was heightened by the economic crisis of 1923.
i. Wall Street Exchange crashed in 1929.
iii. By 1932, industrial production was reduced to 40 percent

questions.question:
Arrange the following in correct sequence with respect to the period in which the event occurred:

  1. The Allies, strengthened by the US entry.
  2. Germany surrendered to the Allies.
  3. Allies were joined by the USSR and the USA.
  4. The Allies defeated Germany and the Central Powers.
questions.explanation:
i. The Allies, strengthened by the US entry in 1917
iv. The Allies defeated Germany and the Central Powers in November 1918.
iii. In 1941, Allies were joined by the USSR and USA.
ii. May 1945, Germany surrendered to the Allies.

questions.question:
Arrange the following in correct sequence with respect to the period in which the event occurred:

  1. Fire Decree declared
  2. Second World War
  3. Enabling Act was passed
  4. Hitler became Chancellor of Germany
questions.explanation:
ii. 1st September 1929 - Second World War
iv. 30th January 1933 - Hitler became Chancellor of Germany
i. 28th February 1933 - Fire Decree declared
iii. 3rd March 1933 - Enabling Act was passed

questions.question:
Arrange the following in correct sequence with respect to the period in which the event occurred:

  1. Adolf Hitler born in Austria
  2. Troops of USSR liberate Auschwitz
  3. Mass murder of Jews Begins
  4. Weimar Republic established
questions.explanation:
i. 1889 - Adolf Hitler born in Austria
iv. 1918 - Weimar Republic established
iii. 1941 - Mass murder of Jews Begins
ii. 1945 - Troops of USSR liberate Auschwitz

questions.question:
Arrange the following in correct sequence with respect to the period in which the event occurred:

  1. Japan bombs Pearl Harbour
  2. Troops of USSR liberate Auschwitz
  3. Mass murder of Jews Begins
  4. Germany surrenders to the Allies
questions.explanation:
iii. 23rd June, 1941 - Mass murder of Jews Begins
i. 7th Dec, 1941 - Japan bombs Pearl Harbour
ii. 27th Jan, 1945 - Troops of USSR liberate Auschwitz
iv. May, 1945 - Germany surrenders to the Allies

questions.question: Which forest law categorized the forest in three categories during colonial rule?
Name the river which flows across Bastar from east to west?
questions.explanation: Indrawati

questions.question: Name the British official who killed 400 tigers during colonial rule?
questions.explanation: George Yule

questions.question: Which one of the following options best signifies this photo?

options.html: Burning the garden penda or podu plot.
Burning the forest Andaman and Nicobar plot.
Burning the forest Dadra or Nagar plot.
Burning the forest penda or podu plot.

questions.explanation: In shifting cultivation, a clearing is made in the forest, usually on the slopes of hills. After the trees have been cut, they are burnt to provide ashes. The seeds are then scattered in the area, and left to be irrigated by the rain.

questions.question: Which one of the following options best signifies this picture?

options.html: Log yard in Rembang under British colonial rule.
Log yard in Rembang under French colonial rule.
Log yard in Rembang under Dutch colonial rule.
Log yard in Rembang under American colonial rule.

questions.explanation: Log yard in Rembang under Dutch colonial rule.

questions.question: Which of the following aspect best signifies this image of shipment?
questions.explanation: Indian Munitions Board, War Timber Sleepers piled at Soolay pagoda ready for shipment, 1917

questions.question: Which of the following aspect best signifies this image?
questions.explanation: Count the dead tigers in the photo. When British colonial officials and Rajas went hunting they were accompanied by a whole retinue of servants. Usually, the tracking was done by skilled village hunters, and the Sahib simply fired the shot.

questions.question: Which one of the following options best signifies this?

options.html: Bamboo rafts being floated down the Brahmaputra river, Chail Hill.
Bamboo rafts being floated down the Yamuna river, Vindhyan hill.
Bamboo rafts being floated down the Narmada river, Amarkantak range.
Bamboo rafts being floated down the Kassalong river, Chittagong Hill Tracts.

questions.explanation: Bamboo rafts being floated down the Kassalong river, Chittagong Hill Tracts.

questions.question: Which one of the following options best signifies this poster?
questions.explanation: European advertisement: Pleasure Brand Tea

questions.question: Which one of the following options best signifies this photograph?

options.html: One aisle of a managed poplar forest in Birmingham, England.
One aisle of a managed poplar forest in Tuscany, Spain.
One aisle of a managed poplar forest in Tuscany, Italy.
One aisle of a managed poplar forest in Berlin, Germany.

questions.explanation: Poplar forests are good mainly for timber. They are not used for leaves, fruit or other products. Look at the straight lines of trees, all of a uniform height. This is the model that ‘scientific’forestry has promoted.

questions.question: Which one of the following options best signifies this building?

options.html: The Imperial Forest School, Delhi, India.
The Imperial Forest School, Dehra Dun, India.
The Imperial Valley School, Dehra Dun, India.
The Queens Valley School, Himachal, India.

questions.explanation: The first forestry school to be inaugurated in the British Empire.

questions.question: Which one of the following options best signifies this Image?
questions.explanation: This photograph of an army camp was taken in Bastar in 1910. The army moved with tents, cooks, and soldiers. Here a sepoy is guarding the camp against rebels.

questions.question: Which of the following aspect best signifies this image of forest?

options.html: Collecting mahua (Madhuca indica) from the forests

questions.explanation: Villagers wake up before dawn and go to the forest to collect the mahua flowers which have fallen on the forest floor. Mahua trees are precious. Mahua flowers can be eaten or used to make alcohol. The seeds can be used to make oil.

questions.question: Which of the following aspect best signifies this image of Plantation?
questions.explanation: A deodar plantation in Kangra.

questions.question: Which of the following aspect best signifies this painting creator?
questions.explanation: Painting by John Dawson.

  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Which of the following aspect best signifies this image of Jungle?

options.html: A mangrove forest in Uttar pradesh

questions.explanation: The different heights of the trees and plants in this picture, and the variety of species. This is a dense forest, so very little sunlight falls on the forest floor.

questions.question: The 1878 forest act divided the forest into how many categories?
questions.explanation: The 1878 forest act divided the forest into 3 categories namely reserved, protected and village forests. Villagers could not take anything from these forests. For house building and fuel they could take wood from protected and village forests.

questions.question: Which community was famous for forest cutters and sifting agriculture?
questions.explanation: The Kalanga of Java were a community of skilled forest cutters and sifting cultivation.they were so valuable that when the Mataram kingdom of Java split the 6000 family members were split equally between both the kingdoms without them it would have been difficult to harvest teak and build the king's palaces.

questions.question: Which is the main reason for survival of some of the dense forests in India?

options.html: Vilages protected them in sacred grooves

questions.explanation: The main reason of survival of some of the dense forest between Kerala and Mizoram is because people protected them in sacred grooves sacred grove or sacred woods are any grove of trees that are of special religious importance to a particular culture. Cutting and hunting is prohibited in these forest. Some villages patrol their own forest.

questions.question: Every year the headmen of villages in a ________ meet and discuss the issue of concern, including forests.
questions.explanation: Every year the headmen of villages in a Pargana meet and discuss the issue of concern, including forests.

questions.question: In Assam men and women were forced to work in which area?
questions.explanation: In Assam both men and women from forest community like the Santhals were recruited to work in tea plantations

questions.question: Alluri Sitarama Raju belonged to ________.
questions.explanation: Alluri Sitarama Raju belonged to Andhra Pradesh

questions.question: Sacred groves are known as ________.
questions.explanation: Sacred groves are known as sarnas,devarakudu,kan,Rai in many parts of India

questions.question: Java is an island in ________.
questions.explanation: Java is an island in Indonesia

questions.question: In which year the Bastar revolution took place
questions.explanation: The people of  the Bastar launched a revolt against the british administration when they banned shifting cultivation,hunting and gathering and instead introduced reserved forest acts which affected the lives of the people in the area.

questions.question:
Identify the correct statements about Java

  1. Java is an island situated in Indonesia.
  2. Java is famous as a wheat-producing island.
  3. The colonial power in Indonesia was the British.
  4. Java in Indonesia is where the Dutch started forest management.
questions.explanation:
Java is famous as a rice-producing island.
The colonial power in Indonesia was the Dutch.

questions.question:
Identify the correct statements about Brandis

  1. Dietrich Brandis suggested that a proper system had to be followed.
  2. Brandis set up the Indian Forest Service in 1865.
  3. Scientific forestry was taught at the Imperial Forest Research Institute.
  4. In Scientific forestry, natural forests which had a variety of trees were cut down and, instead, one type of tree was planted.
questions.explanation: Brandis set up in 1864 the Indian Forest Service

questions.question:
Identify the correct statements about world war

  1. The First World War and the Second World War had a major impact on forests.
  2. In Java, the Dutch followed “a scorched earth” policy.
  3. The Japanese exploited the forests recklessly for their own war industries.
  4. The Japanese destroyed sawmills, and burnt huge piles of giant teak logs so that they would not fall into Dutch hands.
questions.explanation: The Dutch destroyed sawmills, and burnt huge piles of giant teak logs so that they would not fall into Japanese hands.

questions.question:
Identify the correct statements about Samin

  1. Around 1890, Surontiko Samin began questioning state ownership of the forest.
  2. Surontiko Samin resided in Bastar village.
  3. Samin argued that the state had not created the wind, water, earth and wood, so it could not own it.
  4. The Saminists protested by lying down on their land when the Dutch came to survey it.
questions.explanation: Surontiko Samin resided in Randublatung village.

questions.question:
Identify the correct statements about Bastar

  1. Bastar is located in the westernmost part of Chhattisgarh.
  2. Bastar borders Andhra Pradesh, Orissa and Maharashtra.
  3. The central part of Bastar is on a plain.
  4. The river Indrawati winds across Bastar east to west.
questions.explanation:
Bastar is located in the southernmost part of Chhattisgarh.
The central part of Bastar is on a plateau.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question:
Identify the correct statements about the Forest Act

  1. The 1878 Act divided forests into two categories.
  2. The best forests were called reserved forests.
  3. For house building or fuel, people could take wood from protected or village forests.
  4. The Forest Act was enacted in 1865 and it was never amended.
questions.explanation:
The 1878 Act divided forests into three categories: reserved, protected and village forests.
The Forest Act was enacted in 1865 and it was amended twice.

questions.question:
Identify the correct statements about Forest Service

  1. Brandis set up the Indian Forest Service in 1864.
  2. Brandis helped formulate the Indian Forest Act of 1965.
  3. The Imperial Forest Research Institute was set up at Dehradun in 1906.
  4. The system taught in the Institute was called agroforestry.
questions.explanation:
• Brandis helped formulate the Indian Forest Act of 1865. 
  • The system taught in the Institute was called scientific forestry.

questions.question:
Identify the correct statements about Railways

  1. The spread of railways in the 1850s created a new demand.
  2. Railways were essential for colonial trade and for the movement of imperial troops.
  3. To run locomotives, only diesel was needed as fuel.
  4. To lay railway lines sleepers were essential to hold the tracks together.
questions.explanation: C is wrong. To run locomotives, wood was needed as fuel.

questions.question:
Identify the correct statements about Deforestation

  1. Deforestation is a recent problem.
  2. The disappearance of forests is referred to as afforestation.
  3. The process of deforestation began many centuries ago.
  4. Under colonial rule, deforestation became more systematic and extensive.
questions.explanation: C and D

questions.question:
Identify the incorrect statements about Britishers:

  1. The British were worried that the use of forests by local people and the reckless felling of trees by traders would destroy forests.
  2. Britishers decided to invite a German expert, Dietrich Brandis for advice.
  3. Britishers made him the first superintendent of Forests in India.
  4. Britishers made him the first Inspector General of Forests in India.

options.html: Brandis realised that a proper system had to be introduced to manage the forests.
Britishers decided to invite a German expert, Dietrich Brandis for advice.
Britishers made him the first superintendent of Forests in India.
The British were worried that the use of forests by local people and the reckless felling of trees by traders would destroy forests.

questions.explanation: Britishers made him the first Inspector General of Forests in India.

questions.question:
To conserve the forests :
A. the people who live near the forests must be involved.
B. The people who live in forests must be displaced.

options.html: A is false but B is true.
A is true but B is false.
Both A and B are false.
Both A and B are true.

questions.explanation: Conservation of forests rather than collecting timber has become a more important goal. The government has recognised that in order to meet this goal, the people who live near the forests must be involved.

questions.question:
Match the following :

A. Gunda Dhir 1. Indonesia
B. Surontiko Samin 2. Java
C. Kalangs 3. Bastar
D. Dhurwas 4. Kanger forests
questions.explanation:
A.Although there was no single leader, many people speak of Gunda Dhur, from village Nethanar, as an important figure in the movement in Bastar.

B.The Saminist Movement started in the last decade of the 19th century in Java. Surontiko Samin of Randublatung village, a teak forest village headed the movement.

C.The Kalangs of Java were a community of skilled forest cutters and shifting cultivators.

D.The initiative regarding issues of reservation of forest in Bastar was taken by the Dhurwas of the Kanger forest, where reservation first took place.

questions.question:
Which of the following method/s was/were used by the Samin’s to revolt against the Dutch?
A. They lay down on their forest land when the Dutch came to survey it.
B. They refused to pay taxes.

options.html: A is false but B is false.
Both A and B are false.
A is true but B is true.
Both A and B are true.

questions.explanation: Some of the Saminists protested by lying down on their land when the Dutch came to survey it, while others refused to pay taxes or fines or perform labour.

questions.question: Which of the following statement is not correct about the scorched earth policy?

options.html: Under this policy, sawmills were destroyed
It was a policy followed by the Japanese
Huge piles of giant teak logs were burnt

questions.explanation: In Java, just before the Japanese occupied the region, the Dutch followed ‘a scorched earth’ policy, destroying sawmills, and burning huge piles of giant teak logs so that they would not fall into Japanese hands.

questions.question: With which of the following country the samin forest movement is associated?
questions.explanation: The Samin Movement is an Indonesian nationalist movement founded by Surontiko Samin in north-central Java.

questions.question: He argued that the state had not created the wind, water, earth, and wood, so it could not own it. Who is ‘he’ in the above statement?
questions.explanation: Around 1890, Surontiko Samin of Randublatung village, a teak forest village, began questioning state ownership of the forest. He argued that the state had not created the wind, water, earth, and wood, so it could not own it.

questions.question:
Which of the following statement is incorrect about the blandongdiensten system?

  1. It was a kind of rent on cultivated land in the forest
  2. It was rent exemption for the forest villagers
  3. Villagers worked collectively to provide free labour
  4. Villagers gave their buffaloes for cutting and transporting timber

options.html: Villagers gave their buffaloes for cutting and transporting timber
Villagers worked collectively to provide free labour
It was rent exemption for the forest villagers
It was a kind of rent on cultivated land in the forest

questions.explanation: The Dutch first imposed rents on land being cultivated in the forest and then exempted some villages from these rents if they worked collectively to provide free labour and buffaloes for cutting and transporting timber. This was known as the blandongdiensten system. Later, instead of rent exemption, forest villagers were given small wages, but their right to cultivate forest land was restricted.

questions.question: Who were Kalangs?

options.html: They were a community of skilled forest-cutters and shifting cultivators.
They were people of the noble class of Indonesia.

questions.explanation: The Kalangs of Java were a community of skilled forest cutters and shifting cultivators. They were so valuable that in 1755 when the Mataram kingdom of Java split, the 6,000 Kalang families were equally divided between the two kingdoms.

questions.question: Which of the following colonial power controlled Indonesia?
questions.explanation: Once upon a time, Java was covered mostly with forests. The colonial power in Indonesia was the Dutch.

questions.question: Which of the following is the most important crop of Java?
questions.explanation: Java is now famous as a rice-producing island in Indonesia.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question:
What was the result of Bastar Forest Rebellion ?
A. Work on reservation of forests was temporarily suspended.
B. The area to be reserved was reused to roughly half.
C. The colonial government stopped interfering in the management of forests.
questions.explanation: In a major victory for the Bastar rebels, work on reservation was temporarily suspended,and the area to be reserved was reduced to roughly half of that planned before 1910.But the story of the forests and people of Bastar does not end there.After Independence, the same practice of keeping people out of theforests and reserving them for industrial use continued.

questions.question: Which of the following method was used by the people of Bastar to invite villagers to rebel against the British?

options.html: They started firing in the air.
They circulated margo boughs, a lump of earth, chilies, and arrows.

questions.explanation: In 1910, mango boughs, a lump of earth, chilies, and arrows, began circulating between villages. These were actually messages inviting villagers to rebel against the British.

questions.question: Who was Gunda Dhur?

options.html: He was nationalist who lead the Bastar Forest rebellion.

questions.explanation: Although there was no single leader, many people speak of Gunda Dhur, from village Nethanar, as an important figure in the movement. He was a nationalist who leads the Bastar Forest rebellion.

questions.question:
Which of the following event prompted the people of Bastar to rebell against the colonial government ?
A. The proposal to ban hunting.
B. The proposal to reserve two-thirds of the forests.
questions.explanation: When the colonial government proposed to reserve two-thirds of the forest in 1905, and stop shifting cultivation, hunting and collection of forest produce, the people of Bastar were very worried. And hence event prompted the people of Bastar to rebell against the colonial government.

questions.question:
Which of the following is true with reference to the people of Bastar ?
A. They show respect to the spirit of the river, forests and the mountain.
B. They supported the British when the government decided to reserve two-thirds of the forests.

options.html: Both A and B are false.
A is true but B is false.
Both A and B are true.
A is false but B is true.

questions.explanation:
A.In addition to the Earth, they show respect to the spirits of the river,the forest and the mountain.

B.When the colonial government proposed to reserve two-thirds of the forest in 1905, and stop shifting cultivation, hunting and collection of forest produce, the people of Bastar were very worried.They were against British.

Hence A is true but B is false.

questions.question: Where is Bastar Located?
questions.explanation: Bastar is located in the southernmost part of Chhattisgarh.

questions.question: The Forest Act meant severe hardship for the villagers across the country, because:

options.html: Women who collected fire wood were harassed by guards.
Cutting wood, grazing cattle, collecting fruit, roots, hunting and fishing became illegal.
People were forced to steal and if caught, and they had to pay bribes to the forest guards.

questions.explanation:
The Forest Act meant severe hardship for villagers across the country.
After the Act, all their everyday practices – cutting wood for their houses, grazing their cattle, collecting fruits and roots, hunting and fishing – became illegal.
People were now forced to steal wood from the forests, and if they were caught, they were at the mercy of the forest guards who would take bribes from them.
Women who collected fuelwood were especially worried. It was also common for police constables and forest guards to harass people by demanding free food from them.

questions.question:
Which of the following is true with reference to hunting under the colonial rulers-
A. They banned hunting by the locals to protect the wildlife.
B. But they gave rewards for the killings of tigers, wolves and other large animals on the grounds that large animals on the grounds that they posed a threat to cultivators.

options.html: Both A and B are false.
Both A and B are true.
A is false but B is true.
A is true but B is false.

questions.explanation:
A.Under colonial rule the scale of hunting increased to such an extent that various species became almost extinct.

B.They gave rewards for the killing of tigers, wolves and other large animals on the grounds that they posed a threat to cultivators. 

Hence A is false but B is true.

questions.question: Under the colonial government scale of hunting
questions.explanation: Under colonial rule, the scale of hunting increased to such an extent that various species became almost extinct.

questions.question:
European colonial government decided to ban shifting cultivation. Which of the following is/are appropriate reason/s for the same?

  1. They felt that land which was used for cultivation every year could not grow trees for railway timber.
  2. Shifting cultivation made it harder for the government to calculate taxes.
  3. The government wanted to protect the rights of the forest people.
questions.explanation: European foresters regarded this practice as harmful for the forests. They felt that land which was used for cultivation every few years could not grow trees for railway timber. Shifting cultivation also made it harder for the government to calculate taxes. Therefore, the government decided to ban shifting cultivation. As a result, many communities were forcibly displaced from their homes in the forests.

questions.question:
Match the following with reference to shifting agriculture-

(a) Chitemene (i) Southeast Asia
(b) Lading (ii) Central America
(c) milpa (iii) India
(d) Jhum (iv) Africa
questions.explanation: Shifting cultivation or swidden agriculture is a traditional agricultural practice in many parts of Asia, Africa and South America. It has many local names such as lading in Southeast Asia, milpa in Central America, chitemene or tavy in Africa, and Jhum in India.

questions.question: Choose the appropriate term: Under this parts of forests are cut and burnt and the land is cleared for agriculture. The farmers shift to another forest when the land loses fertility.
questions.explanation: In shifting cultivation, parts of the forest are cut and burnt in rotation. Seeds are sown in the ashes after the first monsoon rains, and the crop is harvested by October-November. Such plots are cultivated for a couple of years and then left fallow for 12 to 18 years for the forest to grow back.

questions.question: Which of the following was not an important factor responsible for deforestation during the colonial era?
questions.explanation: The factors which are responsible for deforestation during the colonial era are the commercialization of agriculture, the plantation industry, expansion of the railway. Local people were not responsible for deforestation during the colonial era.

questions.question:
Match the columns:

(a) Reserved forests (i) One type of system of cutting trees.
(b) Protected Forests (ii) No one was allowed to access these forests.
(c) Scientific forestry (iii) Villagers were allowed to collect wood.
questions.explanation:
The 1878 Act divided forests into three categories: reserved, protected, and village forests:

  1. Villagers could not take anything from reserved forests even for their own.
  2. For house building or fuel, they could take wood from protected or village forests.
  3. Scientific forestry: It is a system of cutting trees controlled by the forest department, in which old trees are cut and new ones planted

questions.question:
Which of the following is true with reference to type of forests under the colonial government ?
A. The best forests were called protected forests.
B. Village were not allowed to take anything from the reserved forests.

options.html: A is false but B is true.
A is true but B is false.
Both A and B are false.
Both A and B are true.

questions.explanation:
The best forests were called ‘reserved forests’. Villagers could not take anything from these forests, even for their own use. 

Hence A is false but B is true.
