questions.question:
How is growth of population expressed ?
A. It can be expressed in terms of absolute number.
B. It can be expressed in terms of percentage change per year.
questions.explanation: Growth of population refers to the change in the number of inhabitants of a country/territory during a specific period of time, say during the last ten years. Such a change can be expressed in two ways: in terms of absolute numbers and in terms of percentage change per year.

questions.question: Choose the right word: To change in the number of inhabitants of country/territory during a specific period of time.
questions.explanation:
Growth of population refers to the change in the number of inhabitants of a country/territory during a specific period of time, say during the last ten years. Such a change can be expressed in two ways:

  1. in terms of absolute numbers.
  2. in terms of percentage change per year.

questions.question: Which of the following is not responsible for making the population a dynamic phenomenon?
questions.explanation: The population is a dynamic phenomenon. The numbers, distribution, and composition of the population are constantly changing. This is the influence of the interaction of the three processes, namely-births, deaths, and migrations. Hence, education is not responsible.

questions.question: Which country has the most population in the world?
questions.explanation: In terms of population size, China is the most populated country.

questions.question: Which state has the lowest density of population?
questions.explanation: Arunachal Pradesh has the lowest density of population with only 13person per sq km.

questions.question: Which state has the highest density of population?
questions.explanation: West Bengal has the highest density which is 904 persons per sq km.

questions.question: What was the density of population of India in 2001?
questions.explanation: Density of the population is defined as the number of persons per sq.km. The population density of India in 2001 was 324 persons per sq. km.

questions.question: How is the density of population calculated?
questions.explanation: To calculate the population density, the population is divided by the size of the area.

questions.question: Which is the largest state of India in terms of area?
questions.explanation: Rajasthan is the largest state in India (in terms of area). It covers an area of 342, 239 square kilometres. This represents 10.4% of the whole India.

questions.question:
Sikkim, Arunachal Pradesh, and Jammu & Kashmir have a very low density of population. Which of the following is/are the reason/reasons for the same?

  1. Hilly Dissected and rocky nature of the terrain.
  2. Moderate to low rainfall.
  3. Shallow and less fertile soil.
  4. Low literacy level.
questions.explanation: Sikkim, Arunachal Pradesh, Jammu Kashmir, Assam and most of the Peninsular states have moderate population densities. Hilly, dissected, and rocky nature of the terrain, moderate to low rainfall, shallow, and less fertile soils have influenced population densities in these areas.

questions.question: Which is the least populous state of India?
questions.explanation: Sikkim is India's least populous state, with 610,577(0.6 million) inhabitants according to the 2011 census. Sikkim is also one of the least densely populated Indian states, with only 86 persons per square kilometre.

questions.question: Which is the most populous state of India?
questions.explanation: As of 2011, with an estimated population of 199,581,477. Uttar Pradesh is the most populous state in India. Uttar Pradesh occupies 6.88 percent of India's land surface area but is home to 16.49 percent of India's population.

questions.question: What is the total area of India?
questions.explanation: India is the seventh-largest country in the world, with a total area of 3.28 million square kilometres.

questions.question: What is India’s share in the world population?
questions.explanation: India's population is equivalent to 17.7% of the total world population.

questions.question: Which of the following provides us information regarding the population of our country?
questions.explanation: The census of India is an official count or survey, especially of a population. Thus, it provides information about the population.

questions.question: What was India’s population as per the 2001 census?
questions.explanation: India’s population has been steadily increasing from 361 million in 1951 to 1028 million in 2001.

questions.question: According to the Census 2001, a 'literate person' is one

options.html: who can read and write his and her name in any language
who can read and write in any language with understanding
who is 7 years and above, and can read and write in any language with understanding
who can read and write at least in one language with understanding

questions.explanation: who can read and write at least in one language with understanding

questions.question: The magnitude of 'population growth' refers to

options.html: the total population of an area
the number of females per thousand males
the number of persons added each year
the rate at which the population decreases

questions.explanation: the number of persons added each year

questions.question: Which major section of the population needs 'greater attention' according to the NPP 2000?
questions.explanation: NPP 2000 identified adolescents as one of the major sections of the population that need greater attention. Besides nutritional requirements, the policy put greater emphasis on other important needs of adolescents including protection from unwanted pregnancies and sexually transmitted diseases (STD).

questions.question: Activities such as transport, communication, commerce, administration, etc., are included in
questions.explanation: Tertiary activities consist of industries which provide a service. It includes transport, communications, commerce, administration and other services.

questions.question: Kerala has a sex ratio of
questions.explanation: Sex ratio is defined as the number of females per 1000 males in the population.As per population census, Kerala has the highest sex ratio. It has 1,058 females per 1,000 males.

questions.question: Census provides us with the information regarding
questions.explanation: Census is an official count or survey, especially of a population. Thus it provides all the information regarding the population.

questions.question: According to the Census 2001, a literate person is one who

options.html: can read and write any language
can read and write his/her name
is 7-year-old and can read and write any language with understanding

questions.explanation:
Literacy is a very important quality of a
population.According to the Census of 2001, a person aged 7 years and above who can read and write with understanding in any language, is treated as literate.

questions.question: The magnitude of population growth refers to:

options.html: the number of females per thousand males
the number of persons added each year
the total population of an area
the rate at which the population increases

questions.explanation: Growth of population refers to the change in the number of inhabitants of a country/territory during a specific period of time, say during the last ten years.The total population of an area is the magnitude of population growth.

questions.question: A large proportion of children in a population is a result of:
questions.explanation: Birthrate is a major component of growth because in India, birth rates have always been higher than death rates.High Birth Rate is responsible for a large proportion of children in a population.

questions.question: Migrations change the number, distribution and composition of the population in:

options.html: both the area of departure and arrival

questions.explanation: Migration is an important determinant of population change. It changes not only the population size but also the population composition of urban and rural populations both terms of area of departure and arrival.

questions.question: Which is the largest state of India in terms of area?
questions.explanation: Rajasthan is the largest state in India (in terms of area). It covers an area of 342, 239 square kilometres. This represents 10.4% of the whole India.

questions.question: Which of the following is not responsible for making the population a dynamic phenomenon?
questions.explanation: The population is a dynamic phenomenon. The numbers, distribution, and composition of the population are constantly changing. This is the influence of the interaction of the three processes, namely-births, deaths, and migrations. Hence, education is not responsible.

questions.question: What is the birth rate?

options.html: Number of births per thousand persons in a year.
Sum of birth rate and death rate.
Number of births per ten thousand persons in a year.
Difference between birth rate and death rates.

questions.explanation: The birth rate is the number of live births per thousand persons in a year. It is a major component of growth because, in India, birth rates have always been higher than death rates.

questions.question:
In which of the following age group the dependency ratio will be the highest ?
A. Below 15 years
B. Between 15-59 years
questions.explanation:
Children (generally below 15 years) :They are economically unproductive and need to be provided with food, clothing, education and medical care.

Working Age (15-59 years) :They are economically productive and biologically reproductive. They comprise the working population.

The percentage of children and the aged affect the dependency ratio because these groups are not producers.

questions.question: Why are farmers in Palampur able to grow three different crops in a year?
questions.explanation: People in Palampur are able to grow three different crops because they have electricity and well-developed irrigation system. In the beginning, the tube wells were installed by the government and gradually many private tube wells were also installed.

questions.question: Which of the following is a non-farming activity in Palampur?
questions.explanation: All of these

questions.question: What is Multiple Cropping?

options.html: Growing Different crop by different People
Growing more than one Crop on a piece of same land during the year

questions.explanation: Growing more than one Crop on a piece of same land during the year.

questions.question: Wheat is a
questions.explanation: Rabi Crop

questions.question: Which of the following is a requirement for the production of goods and services?
questions.explanation: All of these

questions.question: Which of these states adopted modern methods of farming at the earliest?

options.html: Uttar Pradesh, Bihar and West Bengal

questions.explanation: Punjab, Haryana and Uttar Pradesh were the first to adopt modern methods of farming.

questions.question: Which one of the following is not an effect of the modern farming?
questions.explanation:
Effects of modern farming techniques:

  • It typically requires the use of chemical fertilizers, resulting degradation of soil quality.
  • The rain dissolves about half of the nitrate in chemical fertilizers used on crops. This pollutes water courses as the dissolved nitrate runs off the lands.
  • In some places, water supplies are inadequate, and modern farming methods require excessive water usage, which will eventually result in water shortages and groundwater depletion.

questions.question: How many private dispensary are there in Palampur?
questions.explanation: One

questions.question: The houses of dalits in Palampur are made up of:
questions.explanation: The SCs (dalits) comprise one-third of the population and live in one corner of the village and in much smaller houses some of which are of mud and straw.

questions.question: Where did the dalits in Palampur live?

options.html: At one corner of the village
In the big hall of the village
In the centre of the village

questions.explanation: The dalits in the Palampur live at the one end of the village.

questions.question: What is the aim of production?
questions.explanation: The aim of production is to produce goods and services.

questions.question: Which Indian village resembles Palampur?
questions.explanation: Village of Western U. P. resemble Palampur.

questions.question: How many populations in Palampur is covered by scheduled caste?
questions.explanation: One-third of the population in Palampur is covered by schedule caste.

questions.question: Who runs the primary health care centre in Palampur?
questions.explanation: The primary health care centre in Palampur is run by the government.

questions.question: How many high schools are there in Palampur?
questions.explanation: One

questions.explanation: Two

questions.explanation: Bullock carts, tongas, bogeys (wooden cart drawn by buffalos).

questions.question: A farmer who works on a piece of 1 hectare of land is treated a
questions.explanation: A farmer who works on a piece of 1 hectare of land is treated as small farmer.

questions.question: Golden Revolution is related to
questions.explanation: Vegetables Production

questions.question: Which sector includes Agriculture and Animal Husbandry?
questions.explanation: Primary Sector include the activities related to agriculture.

questions.question: What is done to surplus wheat in Palampur?
questions.explanation: When ever there was surplus wheat in palampur, it was sold in market.

questions.question: Modern farming methods were tried in India for the first time in
questions.explanation: The modern farming methods are used to increase the yield and save the time of farmers. There are many modern technologies and methods available in modern farming that includes tractors, high yielding variety seeds, pump sets.

questions.question: Which of the following is grown in the rainy season?
questions.explanation: Jowar and bajra

questions.question: The use of high yields with combinations of HYV (High Yielding Varieties) seeds, irrigation, chemical fertilizers, pesticides, etc. refers to
questions.explanation: Modern cropping

questions.question: Operation Flood is related to
questions.explanation: Milk production

questions.question: From where do most of the small farmers borrow money to arrange for the capital in Palampur?
questions.explanation: Village Moneylender

questions.question: Out of the total cultivated areas in the country, how much area is irrigated today
questions.explanation: less than 40%

questions.question: High yielding variety seeds (HYV) were introduced to Indian farmers as a result of
questions.explanation: The introduction of high-yielding varieties (HYV) of seeds and the increased quality of fertilizers and irrigation techniques led to the increase in production to make the country self-sufficient in food grains, thus improving agriculture in India.

questions.question: Which area in India has a low level of irrigation?
questions.explanation: The major part of the Deccan plateau falls in the leeward side, hence it does not get as much rain as the windward side of the plateau. Coastal regions and river basins get irrigated but the Deccan does not.

questions.question: Scope of farming activity is limited in Palampur due to
questions.explanation: The scope of farming is limited in Palampur because land area is very less.

questions.question: If farmers are unable to obtain capital from their own savings and have to borrow, it is called
questions.explanation: If farmers are unable to obtain capital from their own savings and have to borrow, it is called lack of surplus.

questions.question: The new way of farming need
questions.explanation: Working capital

questions.question: Which of the following is not a factor of production?
questions.explanation: Every production is organised by combining land, labour, physical capital and human capital, which are known as factors of production.

questions.question: HYV means:
questions.explanation: HYV stands for High Yielding Variety

questions.question: Which Kharif crop is grown during the rainy season in Palampur?
questions.explanation: Jowar and Bajra are the Kharif crops that are grown during the rainy season in Palampur.

questions.question: Raw material and money in hand are called:
questions.explanation: Raw material and money in hand are called Working Capital.

questions.question: If more than two crops are grown on the same piece of land during a year, it is called:
questions.explanation: If more than two crops are grown on the same piece of land during a year, it is called: Multiple Cropping

questions.question: What capital is needed to set up a jaggery manufacturing unit?
questions.explanation: Physical capital, i.e. the variety of inputs required at every stage during production is needed for setting up any manufacturing unit. Physical capital comprises both fixed and working capital. The most important requirement for the jaggery manufacturing unit is sugarcane, mechanical sugarcane crushing machine and land. Tools, machines, buildings form fixed capital while raw materials (which is sugarcane) and money in hand are working capital.

questions.question: Two major factors of production are:
questions.explanation: Land and labour are the two major factors of production, as capital is created by the application of labour on the land and an entrepreneur is a human resource who performs and controls the business. Therefore, land and labour are the primary factors of production, whereas capital and human capital are the secondary factors of production.

questions.question: People of Palampur sell milk in the near by large village named
questions.explanation: People of Palampur sell milk in the nearby large village named Raiganj.

questions.question: The main activity of the village of Palampur is:
questions.explanation: Farming is the main activity of the village of Palampur.

questions.question: What percentage of the total land area is cultivated by Medium and Large farmers?
Multiple cropping means growing
questions.explanation: More than one crop.

questions.explanation: Tubewells

questions.question: From where do most of the small farmers borrow money to arrange for the capital in Palampur?
questions.explanation: Village Moneylender

questions.question: Who can be said to be unemployed in these following instances?

options.html: Jeetu has done M.A but he is not getting jobs.
Neetu earns Rs 400 every day from household works.
Sakal does not want to work.

questions.explanation: Jeetu has done M.A but he is not getting jobs.

questions.question: Which of the following is Incorrect?

options.html: Literacy rate among female is higher than the male.
Literacy rate have increased in India from 1951 to 2019.
Kerala has the highest literacy rate in India.

questions.explanation: Literacy rate among female is higher than the male.

questions.question: Which of the following is correct?

options.html: Education increases the efficiency of government.
Education contributes towards the growth of society.

questions.explanation: All of these

questions.question: Which of the following is a secondary economic activity?
questions.explanation: Manufacturing

questions.question: Which of the following is a tertiary economic activity?
questions.explanation: Trade

questions.question: Which of the following is a primary economic activity?
questions.explanation: Forestry

questions.question: Which capital is superior to other resources like land and physical capital?
questions.explanation: Human capital is in one way superior to other resources like land and physical capital.

questions.question: Women generally look after ________.
questions.explanation: Women generally look after domestic chores.

questions.question: Due to ________ reasons there is a division of labour between men and women in the family.
questions.explanation: Due to historical and cultural reasons, there is a division of labour between men and women in the family.

questions.question: Which plan endeavoured to raise the country's Gross Enrolment Ratio in higher education in the age group of 18 to 23 years?
questions.explanation: The 12th plan endeavoured to raise the country's Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in higher education in the age group of 18 to 23 years to 25.2% by 2017-8 and to reach the target of 30% by 2020-21, which would be broadly in line with the world average.

questions.question: When the youth with matriculation, graduation and post graduation degrees are not able to find job it is called as ________.
questions.explanation: When the youth with matriculation, graduation and post-graduation degrees are not able to find a job it is called as educated unemployment.

questions.question: Which of the following is not true regarding unemployment?

options.html: Unemployment is said to exist when people who are willing to work but cannot find jobs.
Unemployed are considered as an asset.
Increase in unemployment is an indicator of a depressed economy.
Unemployment leads to wastage of manpower resource.

questions.explanation: Unemployed are considered as a liability.

questions.question: In which sector employment is an absence of basic facilities like maternity leave and childcare?
questions.explanation: In this sector, there is an absence of basic facilities like maternity leave, childcare and other social security systems.

questions.explanation: The workforce population includes people from 15 years to 59 years.

questions.question: Which scheme has been implemented to encourage attendance and retention of children and improve their nutritional status?
questions.explanation: Mid-day meal scheme has been implemented to encourage attendance and retention of children and improve their nutritional status.

questions.question: ________ is a significant step towards providing elementary education to all children in the age group of 6-14 years.

questions.question: Increase in unemployment is an indicator of a ________ economy.
questions.explanation: An increase in unemployment is an indicator of a depressed economy.

questions.question: Which new services are now appearing in the tertiary sector?
questions.explanation: In case of the tertiary sector, various new services are now appearing like biotechnology, information technology and so on.

questions.question: In which sector new services like biotechnology and information technology are now appearing?
questions.explanation: In case of the tertiary sector, various new services are now appearing like biotechnology, information technology and so on.

questions.question: In the secondary sector, ________ is the most labor absorbing.
questions.explanation: In the secondary sector, small scale manufacturing is the most labour absorbing.

questions.question: Which of the following option is not true regarding the agricultural sector?

It is the most labor absorbing sector of the economy.
There has been a decline in the dependence of the population on agriculture partly.
It is considered as Secondary sector.

questions.explanation: Agriculture is primary sector.

questions.question: Agriculture is the most ______ sector of the economy.
questions.explanation: Agriculture is the most labour absorbing sector of the economy.

questions.question: Which sector is the most labour the absorbing sector of the economy?
questions.explanation: Agriculture is the most labour absorbing sector of the economy.

questions.question: There is disguised unemployment in the ________ sector.
questions.explanation: There is disguised unemployment in the agriculture sector.

questions.question: The employment structure is characterised by self-employment in the ________.
questions.explanation: The employment structure is characterised by self-employment in the primary sector.

questions.question: What type of unemployment is prevalent in rural areas?
questions.explanation: In case of rural areas, there is seasonal and disguised unemployment

questions.question: Which of the following is not the aim of India’s National policy relating to health?

Special focus on the privileged segment of the population
Special focus on the underprivileged segment of the population
Improving the accessibility of healthcare and family welfare

questions.explanation: India's national policy aims at improving the accessibility of healthcare, family welfare and nutritional service with a special focus on the underprivileged segment of the population.

questions.question: Which aspect is an indispensable basis for realising one’s well-being?
questions.explanation: Health is an indispensable basis for realising one’s well-being.

questions.question: The _______ of a person helps to realise his/her potential and the ability to fight illness.
questions.explanation: The health of a person helps to realise his/her potential and the ability to fight illness.

questions.question: Which element does firm maximize?
questions.explanation: Firm maximise profit

questions.question: Which aspect decides the growth rate of the country?

options.html: Gender ratio among girls and boys

questions.explanation: The quality of the poulation ultimately decides the growth rate of the country

questions.question: Which field attract women the most among the organized sector?
questions.explanation: Among the organised sector, teaching and medicine attract women the most.

questions.question: Which class majority have meager education and low skill formation?
questions.explanation: A majority of women have meagre education and low skill formation.

questions.question: What are the major determinants of the earning of any individual in the market?
questions.explanation: Education and skill are the major determinants of the earning of any individual in the market

questions.question: Market activities involve
questions.explanation: Market activities involve remuneration to anyone who performs i.e., activity performed for pay or profit.

questions.question: Activity performed for pay or profit is termed as?
questions.explanation: Market activities involve remuneration to anyone who performs i.e., activity performed for pay or profit.

questions.question: Economic activities have been divided into which of the two parts?
questions.explanation: Economic activities have two parts: market activities and non-market activities.

questions.question: Tourism and insurance belongs to which sector?
questions.explanation: Tourism and  insurance are included in the tertiary sector.

questions.question: The secondary sector is also called as
questions.explanation: Manufacturing is included in the secondary sector.

questions.question: The primary sector includes which of the following activities?
questions.explanation: Primary sector includes Poultry farming and mining.

tests.name: Online MCQ Test - 04
tests.instructionHtml:
Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Which sector includes mining and quarrying?
questions.explanation: The primary sector includes mining and quarrying.

questions.question: Economic activities by Men and Women are divided into how many sectors?
questions.explanation: The various activities have been classified into three main sectors i.e., primary, secondary and tertiary.

questions.question: Through which factor or factors investment in human capital is done?
questions.explanation: Investment in human capital (through education, training, medical care) yields a return just like investment in physical capital.

questions.question: GNP stands for?
questions.explanation: Gross National Product

questions.question: Which factor turns the population into human capital?
questions.explanation: Population becomes human capital when there is investment made in the form of education, training and medical care.

questions.question: The probability of certain communities becoming or remaining poor in the coming years is called
questions.explanation: Probability of certain communities of becoming or remaining poor in coming years is called Vulnerability to poverty.

questions.question: When people appear to be employed but are actually not employed fully, this kind of unemployment is called:
questions.explanation: When people appear to be employed but are actually not employed fully, this kind of unemployment is called disguised unemployment.

questions.question: What is the aim of the mid-day meal scheme in schools?
questions.explanation: A mid-day meal scheme has been implemented to encourage attendance and retention of children and improve their nutritional status.

questions.question: What is the situation called when there is a surplus of manpower in certain categories and shortage of manpower in other categories.
questions.explanation: Paradoxical manpower situation

questions.question: In the secondary sector which of the following is the most labour absorbing?
questions.explanation: Small scale manufacturing is the most labour absorbing sector of the secondary sector.

questions.question: Which work, done mostly by woman, is not considered in the National Income?

options.html: Work done in a private company

questions.explanation: Women are not paid for the services or household work delivered in the family. So, household work is not considered in the National Income.

questions.question: Which one of these is the most labour absorbing sector of the economy?
questions.explanation: Agriculture is the most labor absorbing sector of the economy.

questions.question: When people appear to be employed, this kind of unemployment is called:
questions.explanation: Disguised unemployment: In this situation, more people are engaged in work than required. They all appear to be employed. It is also called Hidden unemployment.

questions.question: Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan is a significant step towards providing education to all children in the age group:
questions.explanation: It aims to provide compulsory and free elementary education to all children between the age of 6-14 years.

questions.question: An activity performed for profit or for service can be termed as:
questions.explanation: Market activities involve remuneration to anyone who performs i.e., activity performed for pay or profit. These include the production of goods or services, including government service.

tests.instructionHtml:
Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: ________ means that certain groups or communities have a greater probability of either becoming poor or remaining poor in the next few years.
questions.explanation: The phrase ‘vulnerability to poverty’ means that certain groups or communities have a greater probability of either becoming poor or remaining poor in the next few years. The groups which are vulnerable to poverty are the people belonging to scheduled castes, scheduled tribes, rural agricultural labour households and urban casual labour households.

questions.question: Who can be a vulnerable group in India?

options.html: Rural Agricultural labour Household can be a vulnerable group in India
Both Urban casual labour household and Rural Agricultural labour Household

questions.explanation: Among the economic groups, the most vulnerable groups are the rural agricultural labour households and urban casual labour households.

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of MNREGA-2005?

options.html: Unemployment allowance in case of no-work provided

questions.explanation:
The main features of the National Rural Employment Guarantee Act 2005 (NREGA) is as follow:

  • It guarantees the 100 days of wage employment in the financial year to a rural household.
  • If the applicant is not employed within 15 days, then he/she will be entitled to a daily unemployment allowance.

questions.question: Which scheme started in 1993 has the aim to create self-employment opportunities for educated unemployed youth in rural areas and small towns?
questions.explanation: Prime Minister Rozgar Yojana is the scheme which was started in 1993. The aim of the program is to create self-employment opportunities for educated unemployed youth in rural areas and small towns. They help in setting up small businesses and industries.

questions.question: Poverty has resurfaced in which of the following country?
questions.explanation: Poverty has also resurfaced in some of the former socialist countries like Russia, where officially it was non-existent earlier.

questions.question: Which of the following is an anti-poverty strategy of the government?

options.html: Both Promotion of Economic Growth and Targeted anti-poverty programmes

questions.explanation: Both Promotion of Economic Growth and Targeted anti-poverty programmes

questions.question: Poverty line is measured on the basis of:
questions.explanation: Income and Consumption both

questions.question: Which of the following is not a cause of poverty?
questions.explanation: Economic Growth

questions.question: In which year Prime Minister Rozgar Yojana was launched?
questions.explanation: In the year 1993 Prime Minister Rozgar Yojana was launched.

questions.question: Which of the following is the historical reason for the widespread poverty in India?

options.html: Unequal distribution of land and other resources
Low level of economic development under the British colonial administration

questions.explanation: There were a number of causes for the widespread poverty in India. One historical reason is the low level of economic development under the British colonial administration. The policies of the colonial government ruined traditional handicrafts and discouraged the development of industries like textiles.

questions.question: SGSY stands for?
questions.explanation: Swarnajayanti Gram Swarozgar Yojana

questions.question: Average calories requirement in rural India is ________ calories.
questions.explanation: The average calories requirement in rural India 2400 calories.

questions.question: With the spread of ________, many job opportunities were created in the agriculture sector.

options.html: anti poverty schemes and modern technology

questions.explanation: With the spread of irrigation and the Green revolution, many job opportunities were created in the agriculture sector.

questions.question: The current anti-poverty strategy of the government is based broadly on which plank?

options.html: Targeted anti-employment programmes and promotion of economic poverty
Promotion of economic growth and targeted anti-poverty programme

questions.explanation: Removal of poverty has been one of the major objectives of Indian developmental strategy. The current anti-poverty strategy of the government is based broadly on two planks (1) promotion of economic growth (2) targeted anti-poverty programmes.

questions.question: Launched in 1995, which scheme has the aim of creating self-employment opportunities in rural areas and small towns?
questions.explanation: Rural Employment Generation Programme was launched in 1995. The aim of the programme is to create self employment opportunities in rural areas and small towns.

questions.question: Under which scheme additional central assistance is given to states for basic services such as primary health, primary education, etc?
questions.explanation: Under the Pradhan Mantri Gramodaya Yojana launched in 2000, additional central assistance is given to states for basic services such as primary health, primary education, rural shelter, rural drinking water and rural electrification.

questions.question: Which programme aims at bringing the assisted poor families above the poverty line by organising them into self-help groups?
questions.explanation: Swarnajayanti Gram Swarozgar Yojana was launched in 1999. The programme aims at bringing the assisted poor families above the poverty line by organising them into self help groups through a mix of bank credit and government subsidy.

questions.question: Which state has traditionally succeeded in reducing poverty with the help of high agricultural growth rates?
questions.explanation: Haryana has traditionally succeeded in reducing poverty with the help of high agricultural growth rates.

questions.question: Which strategy is adopted by Kerala to remove poverty?
questions.explanation: Kerala has focused more on human resource development.

questions.question: Which state has focused more on human resource development?
questions.explanation: Kerala has focused more on human resource development.

questions.question: NSSO stands for?
questions.explanation: National Sample Survey Organisation

questions.question: A person is considered poor if his or her ________ level falls below a given minimum level necessary to fulfil basic needs.
questions.explanation: A person is considered poor if his or her income or consumption level falls below a given “minimum level” necessary to fulfil basic needs.

questions.question: Sivaraman a cobbler by caste works as an agricultural labourer for Rs 50 per day. But that’s only for five to six months in a year. At other times, he does odd jobs in the town. His son- Karan has completed his graduation but he is still unemployed. Recently, a scheme has been launched in the village. The aim of the programme is to create self-employment opportunities for educated unemployed youth in rural areas and small towns. Karan took advantage of this scheme and is self-employed now. Name the scheme.

options.html: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act.

questions.explanation: Prime Minister Rozgar Yozana (PMRY) is another scheme which was started in 1993. The aim of the programme is to create self-employment opportunities for educated unemployed youth in rural areas and small towns.

questions.question: Tejpal Singh works as a peasant in the village. His income from agricultural production is very less. The money is not enough to sustain his family of six-that includes his wife and four children. His wife- Savita is unemployed and wishes to work for earning extra income. Recently, a new scheme has been launched in the village. Under the scheme, one-third of the proposed jobs have been reserved for women. This scheme helped Savita to get the job in the village. Name the scheme which helped Savita to get a job.

options.html: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act.

questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act, 2005 aims to provide 100 days of wage employment to every household to ensure livelihood security in rural areas. One-third of the proposed jobs have been reserved for women.

questions.question: Ramlal is a villager living in a kacchha house. He was not getting any work leading to the condition of poverty. Subsequently, a new scheme was launched in the village and Ramlal got the assured work of 100 days. Based on the above case, name the scheme launched in the village.

options.html: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act

questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act, 2005 aims to provide 100 days of wage employment to every household to ensure livelihood security in rural areas

questions.question: Shyamlal is a farmer and is engaged in the cultivation of potatoes. When the potatoes are harvested all his production of potatoes is transported to Ramlal who is a factory owner. In the factory, the potatoes are processed into potato chips. Packing is done and transported to the market for sale to consumers. Based on the above case, identify the sector in which Shyamlal is engaged.
questions.explanation: Primary activities are directly dependent on environment as these refer to utilisation of earth’s resources such as land, water, vegetation, building materials and minerals. It, thus includes, hunting and gathering, pastoral activities, fishing, forestry, agriculture, and mining and quarrying.

questions.question: Shyamlal is a farmer and is engaged in the cultivation of sugarcane. When the sugarcane is harvested all his production of sugarcane is transported to Ramlal who is a factory owner. In the factory, the sugarcane is converted into jaggery and transported to market for the sale to consumers. Based on the above case, identify the sector in which Ramlal is engaged.
questions.explanation: Processing of this sugar cane in factories to produce sugar is a secondary sector activity.

questions.question: Thirty-year-old Lakha Singh works as a daily-wage labourer in an urban area. He lives in a one-room rented house in a crowded basti in the outskirts of the city. It’s a temporary shack built of bricks and clay tiles. The money is not enough to sustain his family of six-that includes his wife and four children aged between 12 years to six months. Lakha Singh is unhealthy and not able to work efficiently as his intake of accepted average calorie requirement is very low. With respect to the above case analyse what is the standard accepted average calorie requirement.
questions.explanation: The accepted average calorie requirement in India is 2100 calories per person per day in urban areas. Lakha Singh resides in an urban area his average calorie requirement is 2100 calorie per day.

questions.question: Devilal and his family live in a slum area. It has very poor surroundings with no basic facilities. No rich family resides in such an area. Devilal is bound to live only in a poor surrounding with other poor people and denied from enjoying social equality of better-off people in better surroundings. Find out what situation they are facing there?
questions.explanation: According to this concept, poverty must be seen in terms of the poor having to live only in poor surroundings with other poor people, excluded from enjoying social equality of better-off people in better surroundings.

questions.question: Village Palampur is situated about 10 kilometres west of Surat. In a village, males of families work as labourers while the females are responsible for household jobs. The income of all the families in the village is meagre. Subsequently, the programme was launched by the government. Under this programme, a collective group was formed by 15 women. Each member of the group deposits Rs. 100 as savings every month. To meet their needs, members can take small loans from the group itself or through a mix of bank credit and government subsidy. Based on the above case, name the programme by which government organised women into self-help groups.
questions.explanation: Swarnajayanti Gram Swarozgar Yojana (SGSY) was launched in 1999. The programme aims at bringing the assisted poor families above the poverty line by organising them into self-help groups through a mix of bank credit and government subsidy.

questions.question: Thirty-three year old Ram Saran works as a daily-wage laborer in a wheat flour mill in a rural area of Ranchi in Jharkhand. He manages to earn around Rs 1,500 a month when he finds employment, which is not often. The money is not enough to sustain his family of six- that includes his wife and four children aged between 12 years to six months. Ram Saran is unhealthy and not able to work efficiently as his intake of accepted average calorie requirement is very low. With respect to the above case analyze what is the standard accepted average calorie requirement.
questions.explanation: The accepted average calorie requirement in India is 2400 calories per person per day in rural areas and 2100 calories per person per day in urban areas. Since, Ram saran lives in rural area hence his average calorie requirement is 2400 per day.

questions.question: Consider a case of a small village near Karur town in Tamil Nadu. The village is facing a very poor condition. There are unmetalled roads in the village. Along with this, the village has no access to pure drinking water. There is no clinic or a school in the village. Even people have to live without electricity. Subsequently, in order to curb such conditions, the central government assisted the state government and launched a scheme. After the implementation of the scheme, villagers have access to basic services such as primary health, primary education, rural shelter, rural drinking water, and rural electrification. Give the name of the scheme that lead the village to prosperity.

options.html: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act.

questions.explanation: Under the Pradhan Mantri Gramodaya Yojana (PMGY) launched in 2000, additional central assistance is given to states for basic services such as primary health, primary education, rural shelter, rural drinking water, and rural electrification.

questions.question:
Which of the following group of people are prone to food Insecurity?

  1. SC
  2. ST
  3. OBC
  4. Landless
questions.explanation:
Group of people prone to food Insecurity are

  1. SC
  2. ST
  3. OBC
  4. Landless

questions.question: Which of the following state has the lowest poverty rate?
questions.explanation: Goa has the lowest poverty rate.

questions.question:
Which of the following are the components of human poverty?

  1. Education
  2. Health
  3. Shelter
questions.explanation:
1. Education
  2. Health
  3. Shelter

questions.question: The result of the most of the antipoverty schemes has been mixed. Which of the following are most important reasons for the same ? A. Lack of proper implementation B.  Not right targeting C. Over dependency on agriculture
questions.explanation: Result of the most of the antipoverty schemes has been mixed due to -A Lack of proper implementation B.  Not right targeting

questions.question: Which of the following programme of the government provide self-employment opportunities for educated unemployed youth in rural areas?
questions.explanation: PMRY (Prime Minister Rozgar Yojana) programme of the government provides self-employment opportunities for educated unemployed youth in rural areas.

questions.question: Which programme of the government provides compensation to unemployed if he fails to get 100 days of employment for a year?
questions.explanation: NREGA programme of the government provides compensation to unemployed if he fails to get 100 days of employment for a year.

questions.question: For how many days NRGEA provide employment?
questions.explanation: NRGEA provides employment for 100 days.

questions.question: Which of the following yojna provide additional central assistance to states for basic services such as primary education, health, etc.?
questions.explanation: PMGY (Pradhan Mantri GramodayaYozana) provide additional central assistance to states for basic services such as primary education, health, etc.

questions.question: Which of the following yojna has been launched to bring the assisted poor families above the poverty line by organizing them into self-help groups through a mix of bank credit and government subsidy?
questions.explanation: SGSY(Swarnajayanti Gram Swarozgar Yojana) has been launched to bring the assisted poor families above the poverty line by organizing them into self help groups through a mix of bank credit and government subsidy.

questions.question: Which of the following scheme is to create self-employment opportunities for educated unemployed youth in rural areas and small towns?
questions.explanation: PMRY (Prime Minister Rozgar Yojna) scheme is to create self-employment opportunities for educated unemployed youth in rural areas and small towns.

questions.question: What is MNREGA?

options.html: Marginal Natural Rural Employment Guarantee Act.
Marginal National Rural Employment Guarantee Act.
Mahatama Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act.
Mahatama Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Action.

questions.explanation: MNREGA stands for Mahatama Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (2005).

questions.question: When was NREGA passed?
questions.explanation: NREGA was passed in September 2005.

questions.question: Which of the following country has highest poverty ratio?
questions.explanation: Nigeria has highest poverty ratio. It has 62% of population below $1.25 a day.

questions.question: In which region of the world poverty has risen up?
questions.explanation: The world poverty has risen up in Sub Saharan Africa region.

questions.question: In which of the following country poverty has decreased substantially?
questions.explanation: Poverty in China has reduced substantially, from 85% in 1981 to 6% in 2011.

questions.question: What is global poverty ratio (2001)?
questions.explanation: Global poverty ratio for the year 2001 is 21%.

questions.question: In which of the following countries did poverty actually rise from 1981 to 2001?
questions.explanation: In Sub-Saharan Africa, poverty has risen from 41 percent in 1981 to 46 percent in 2001.

questions.question: In which of the following state Public Distribution System has helped in reducing the poverty?
questions.explanation: In Tamil Nadu public grain distribution system has helped in declining poverty ratio.

questions.question: In which of the following state land reform measures have helped in reducing poverty?
questions.explanation: In West Bengal, land reform measures have helped in reducing poverty.

questions.question: Which of the following states has shown a significant decline in poverty ratio ?
questions.explanation: Kerala and Andhra Pradesh has shown a significant decline in poverty ratio. Kerala has focussed more on human resource development. Andra Pradesh increased food grain distribution.

questions.question: Which of the following state has focused more on human resource development?
questions.explanation: Kerala has focussed more on human resource development. Literacy rate in Kerala was 94% in 2011.

questions.question: Which of the following Indian state has a maximum number of people living below the poverty line?
questions.explanation: Uttar Pradesh has the highest number of people living below the poverty line during 2011-12 at 598.19 people below the poverty line out of every lakh.

questions.question:
The poorest states in India are

  1. Orissa
  2. Bihar
  3. Punjab
  4. Haryana
questions.explanation: Odisha and Bihar are among the poorest states in India.

questions.question: Which of the following social group has not seen a decline in poverty ratio?
questions.explanation: Scheduled Tribes has not seen a decline in poverty ratio.

questions.question: Which of the following is not included in the most vulnerable group of poverty?
questions.explanation: Marginal farmers are not included in the most vulnerable group of poverty because they are cultivating big farms (up to 1 hectare) as owners or landlords.

questions.question: Which of the following group is not a vulnerable group to poverty?
questions.explanation: Upper Caste group is not vulnerable group to poverty.

questions.question: What is NSSO?
questions.explanation: National Sample Survey Organisation (NSSO) carries out sample surveys in India.

questions.question: Under _________ programme, one member of each rural household shall be given 100 days employment in a year.

options.html: Mahatma Gandhi Rural Employment Guarantee Scheme

questions.explanation: MNREGA (Mahatma Gandhi Rural Employment Guarantee Scheme) was originally started in 2005 as NREGA. Under this programme, one member of each rural household shall be given 100 days of employment in a year. It aims to guarantee the 'right to work'.

questions.question: Food Security is ensured by the government in India:

options.html: Both By Public Distribution System and By Buffer Stock

questions.explanation: The food security is ensured in India through a carefully designed food security system by the government. This system has two components: (i) Buffer Stock and (ii) Public Distribution System.

questions.question: Who is food insecure in India?
questions.explanation: In rural areas, the worst affected people like landless and small farmers, traditional artisans (weavers, potters, blacksmith etc.) providers of services e.g. barbers, washermen etc, petty self-employed workers and destitute are Food-insecure

questions.question: Buffer Stock is prepared in India by:
questions.explanation: Buffer stock is the stock of food grains (e.g. wheat,rice etc.) procured by the government through Food Corporation of India (FCI).

questions.question: What is Buffer Stock?

options.html: Stock of Food Grain and Stock of textbooks

questions.explanation: Buffer stock is the stock of food grains (e.g. wheat, rice etc.) procured by the government through Food Corporation of India (FCI).

questions.question: Indira Gandhi released a special stamp entitled ________ in July 1968.
questions.explanation: Indira Gandhi, the then Prime Minister of India, officially recorded the impressive strides of the Green revolution in agriculture by releasing a special stamp entitled 'Wheat Revolution' in July 1968.

questions.question: Which cooperative in Delhi is making strides in provision of milk and vegetables to the consumers at controlled rate?
questions.explanation: In Delhi, Mother Dairy is making strides in the provision of milk and vegetables to the consumers at a controlled rate.

questions.question: Which organisation organises training and capacity building programmes on food security for NGOs?
questions.explanation: Academy of Development Science (ADS) organises training and capacity building programmes on food security for NGOs.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a cooperative?
questions.explanation: TISCO is private sector steel company.

questions.question: Ration shops are also known as ________.
questions.explanation: Ration shops are also known as Fair Price Shops.

questions.question: On what factors food security depends?
questions.explanation: Food security depends on the Public Distribution System (PDS) and government vigilance.

questions.question: Under which system the food procured by the FCI is distributed through government regulated ration shops among the poorer section of the society?
questions.explanation: The food procured by the FCI is distributed through government-regulated ration shops among the poorer section of the society. This is called the public distribution system.

questions.question: PDS stands for?
questions.explanation: Public distribution system

questions.question: The farmers are paid a pre-announced price for their crops. This price is called ________.
questions.explanation: The farmers are paid a pre-announced price for their crops. This price is called Minimum Support Price.

questions.question: ________ is the stock of foodgrains procured by the government through Food Corporation of India.
questions.explanation: Buffer Stock is the stock of foodgrains, namely wheat and rice procured by the government through Food Corporation of India (FCI).

questions.question: MSP stands for?
questions.explanation: Minimum Support Price

questions.question: FCI stand for?
questions.explanation: Food Corporation of India (FCI).

questions.question: Who purchases wheat and rice from the farmers in states where there is surplus production?
questions.explanation: The FCI purchases wheat and rice from the farmers in states where there is surplus production.

questions.question: In which year the most devastating famine occurred in Bengal?
questions.explanation: The most devastating famine that occurred in India was the famine of Bengal in 1943.

questions.question: A massive starvation might take a turn of ________.
questions.explanation: A massive starvation might take a turn of famine.

questions.question: When is food security ensured in a country?

options.html: When all persons have the capacity to buy food of acceptable quality
When enough food is available for all the persons
When there is no barrier on access to food

questions.explanation:
Food security is ensured in a country only if:

  1. enough food is available for all the persons,
  2. all persons have the capacity to buy food of acceptable quality 
  3. there is no barrier in access to food.

questions.question: Where did the most devastating famine occur in India in 1943?
questions.explanation: The most devastating famine that occurred in India was the famine of Bengal in 1943.

questions.question: Which of the following is not the dimension of food security?
questions.explanation:
Food security has the following dimensions:

  1. Availability of food
  2. Accessibility 
  3. Affordability

questions.question: ________ implies that an individual has enough money to buy sufficient, safe and nutritious food to meet one's dietary needs.
questions.explanation: Affordability implies that an individual has enough money to buy sufficient, safe and nutritious food to meet one's dietary needs.

questions.question: ________ means food is within reach of every person.
questions.explanation: Accessibility means food is within reach of every person.

questions.question: ________ of food means food production within the country, food imports and the previous years stock stored in government granaries.
questions.explanation: The availability of food means food production within the country, food imports and the previous years' stock stored in government granaries.

questions.question: Food within the reach of every person is called ________.
questions.explanation: Food within the reach of every person is called Accessibility.

questions.question: Which of the following is/are true with reference to ADS ? A. It has facilitated a network of NGO’s for setting up grain banks in different regions. B. It organisises training and capacity building programmers and food security for NGO’s

options.html: A is false but B is true
Both A and B are true
A is true but B is false
Both A and B are false

questions.explanation:
ADS has

A. It has facilitated a network of NGO’s for setting up grain banks in different regions.--true

B. It organisises training and capacity building programmers and food security for NGO’s---false

questions.question: Which of the following is a cooperative of Delhi?
questions.explanation: Mother dairy is a cooperative of Delhi.

questions.question: Which of the following do not falls under cooperative?
questions.explanation: Reliance does not fall under the category of the cooperative.

questions.question: In which parts of India cooperatives have played an important role in food security in India?
questions.explanation: In Southern and western parts of India, cooperatives have played an important role in food security in India.

questions.question: Which of the following is not playing a role in food security?
questions.explanation: RBI is not playing any role in food security.

questions.question: Which of the following schemes were launched with special target groups of the poorest of the poor and the indigent senior citizens?
questions.explanation: AAY (Antyodaya Anna Yojana) and APS( Annapurna Scheme) schemes were launched with special target groups of  'poorest of the poor ' and ' indigent senior citizens '.

questions.question: Which of the following programmes has been launched by the government to adopt the principle of targeting the poor in all areas?
questions.explanation: TPDS (Targeted Public Distribution System) has been launched by the government to adopt the principle of targeting the poor in all areas.

questions.question: Which of the following is the most important step taken by the Government of India towards ensuring food security?
questions.explanation: PDS is the most important step taken by the Government of India towards ensuring food security.

questions.question: Which of the following was not part of the three important food intervention programmers introduced by the government?
questions.explanation:
PAP was not among the three important food intervention programmers introduced by the government. The three important were:

  1. Public Distribution System (PDS): This system was made to ensure a smooth supply of subsidized food items to the poor.
  2. Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS): These services were introduced to provide proper nutrition to the poor children.
  3. Food For Work (FFW): This programme was introduced to help the poor earn food in return of some work and also to provide them with employment.

questions.question: After which of the following incident the rationing was introduced in India in the 1940’s?
questions.explanation: After Bengal Famine in 1940 the rationing was introduced in India.

questions.question: The shopkeeper stock of food grains, sugar, kerosene, etc. and sell to the poor and a price lower than the market price:
questions.explanation: Fair Price Shops keep stock of food grains, sugar, kerosene, etc. and sell to the poor and a price lower than the market price.

questions.question: What is RPDS?
questions.explanation: RPDS is Revamped Public Distribution System

questions.question: What is PDS?
questions.explanation: PDS is Public Distribution System

questions.question: The food procured by the FCI is distributed through government-regulated ration shops among the poorer section of the society. This is called the ________.
questions.explanation: The food procured by the FCI is distributed through government-regulated ration shops among the poorer section of the society. This is called PDS(Public Distribution System)

questions.question: It is the price at which government provides food grains to the poor strata of the society through the PDS(Public Distribution System).
questions.explanation: Issue price is the price at which government provides food grains to the poor strata of the society through the PDS(Public Distribution System)

questions.question:
Why is buffer stock created by the government ?
A. For food security
B. To resolve the problem of food shortage during adverse conditions.

options.html: Both A and B are false
Both A and B are true
A is true but B is false
A is false but B is true

questions.explanation: Buffer stock created by the government for  For food security and not to to resolve the problem of food shortage during adverse conditions.

questions.question: Buffer stock is the stock of food grains, namely wheat and rice procured by the government through ________.
questions.explanation: Buffer stock is the stock of food grains, namely wheat and rice procured by the government through FCI.

questions.question: Which of the following agency purchases food grains from the farmers?
questions.explanation: FCI (Food Corporation of India) purchases food grains from the farmers.

questions.question: Choose the appropriate word - It is the stock of food grains procured by the government through FCI.
questions.explanation: The stock of food grains procured by the government through FCI is called Buffer Stock.

questions.question: What is the main function of FCI?

options.html: To purchase cash crop from the farmers
To purchase seeds and other inputs to farmers
To purchase wheat and rice from the farmers

questions.explanation: To purchase wheat and rice from the farmers is the main function of FCI

questions.question: What is FCI?
questions.explanation: FCI is Food Corporation of India.

questions.question: What is MSP?
questions.explanation: Minimum Support Price is full form of MSP.

questions.question: What are the two components of food security system?
questions.explanation: Buffer stock, Public distribution system(PDS) are two components of the food security system.

questions.question: Which of the following has helped India in achieving self-sufficiency in food grains?
questions.explanation: Green Revolution helped India in achieving self sufficiency in food grains.

questions.question: Which of the following states achieved the highest growth rate in agriculture during the Green Revolution era?
questions.explanation: Punjab and Haryana states achieved the highest growth rate in agriculture during the Green Revolution.

questions.question: The production of which of the following crops has increased because of the Green Revolution?
questions.explanation: The production of the crops of Rice and wheat has increased because of the Green Revolution.

questions.question: Since Independence, India has been aiming at :
questions.explanation: Since Independence, India has been aiming at: Self-sufficiency in food grains.

questions.question: People suffer from chronic hunger due to:

options.html: Low production of food in the country

questions.explanation: People suffer from chronic hunger due to Low income.

questions.question: Which type of hunger is a consequence of diets persistently inadequate in terms of quantity and quality?

options.html: Fast kept due to spiritual reasons

questions.explanation: Chronic hunger is a consequence of diets persistently inadequate in terms of quantity and quality.

questions.question: Which of the following are two dimensions of hunger?
questions.explanation: Chronic and seasonal, are two dimensions of hunger.

questions.question: Which of the following group of states account for a large number of food-insecure people in the country?

options.html: Jammu and Kashmir, Bihar, West Bengal

questions.explanation: Bihar, Jharkhand, and Orissa, group of states account for a large number of food-insecure people in the country.

questions.question: During natural calamity the production of food ________.
questions.explanation: During natural calamity the production of food decreases.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a component of food security?
questions.explanation: Accountability is not a component of food security.

questions.question: ________ of food means food production within the country, food imports and the previous years stock stored in government granaries.
questions.explanation: The availability of food means food production within the country, food imports and the previous years' stock stored in government granaries.

questions.question: There are 40 villages in a district where the government has made no provision for drinking water. These villagers met and considered many methods of forcing the government to respond to their need. Which of these is not a democratic method?

options.html: Boycotting the next elections to give a message to all parties
Filing a case in the courts claiming that water is part of right to life
Organising public meetings against government’s policies
Paying money to government officials to get water

questions.explanation: Paying money to government officials to get water is surely a non democratic method as it encourages bribery and corruption in society. Such a method deprives other sections of the society of water.

questions.question: Which of these is not a valid reason for arguing that there is a lesser possibility of famine in a democratic country?

options.html: Opposition parties can draw attention to hunger and starvation.
Free press can report suffering from famine in different parts of the country.
Government fears its defeat in the next elections.
People are free to believe in and practise any religion.

questions.explanation: People are free to believe in and practice any religion, is not a valid reason for arguing that there is a lesser possibility of famine in a democratic country.

questions.question: In which of these countries only the ruling party is allowed to contest in an election:
questions.explanation: Mexico

questions.question: Who said democracy is of the people, for the people and by the people?
questions.explanation: Abraham Lincoln

questions.question: Name the leader who brought dictatorship in Germany?
questions.explanation: Hitler

questions.question: ________ has been the President of Zimbabwe since its independence.
questions.explanation: Robert Mugabe has been the President of Zimbabwe since its independence.

questions.question: Democracy is based on the fundamental principle of ________.
questions.explanation: Democracy is based on a fundamental principle of political equality. In a democracy, each adult citizen must have one vote and each vote must have one value.

questions.question: ________ is/are the real source of power in a democratic country.
questions.explanation: People are the real source of power in a democratic country.

questions.question: General Pervez Musharraf issued a Legal Framework Order to amend the constitution of Pakistan in the year ________.
questions.explanation: General Pervez Musharraf issued a Legal Framework Order to amend the constitution of Pakistan in the year 2002.

questions.question: ________ means a rule of the people, for the people and by the people.
questions.explanation: Democracy means rule of the people, for the people and by the people.

questions.question: Augusto Pinochet was the ruler of ________.
questions.explanation: Augusto Pinochet was the ruler of Chile.

questions.question: The parliament of China is known as the ________.
questions.explanation: The parliament of China is known as the National People's Congress.

questions.question: Democracy is a form of government where the people elect their ________.
questions.explanation: representative

questions.question: Democracy originated:

options.html: in the UK, after the signing of Magna Carta.
in the US after freeing itself from British tyranny.

questions.explanation: Full democracy was not established in the US, the UK, or France until the twentieth century.

questions.question: Direct democracy is the system in which:

options.html: citizens are allowed to debate with their representatives in open public meetings.
citizens choose their representatives in free and fair elections.
citizens represent themselves in the decision-making process.
senior political leaders are known as Directors.

questions.explanation: citizens represent themselves in the decision-making process.

questions.question: Which of these is a defining characteristic of democracy?

options.html: That rulers govern in the interests of the ruled
That the proceedings of the legislative body are televised
That rulers come from a wide range of social backgrounds
That rulers are directly accountable to the ruled on a regular basis

questions.explanation: That rulers are directly accountable to the ruled on a regular basis

questions.question: What is the term used to denote regimes which manipulate the electoral process in various ways, without resorting to blatant vote-rigging?
questions.explanation: Illiberal democracy

questions.question: Which of these is not a feature of monarchy, dictatorship or one-party rule?
questions.explanation: Citizens taking part in politics

questions.question: Which body in the Indian political system is an example of direct democracy?
questions.explanation: Gram Sabha

questions.question: Democracy improves the quality of decision-making because:

options.html: All decisions are approved by judiciary
Decisions are taken by consultation and discussion
Decisions are taken by educated people
Decisions are taken over a long period of time

questions.explanation: Decisions are taken by consultation and discussion

questions.question: In which period did China face one of the worst famines that have occurred in the world?
Which of the following is not a drawback of democracy?
questions.explanation: This is an important principle of democracy.

questions.question: Which of these is permitted under the Rule of Law?

options.html: Prime Minister can be punished for violating the Constitution
Police has a right to kill anybody
President can rule for as long as he wants
Women can be paid lesser salaries

questions.explanation: Prime Minister can be punished for violating the Constitution

questions.question: What is Constitutional Law?

options.html: Law which create discrimination on caste
Law to set up Constituent Assembly

questions.explanation: Provisions given in the Constitution

questions.question: Which organ of the government is required to protect the rights of the citizens?
questions.explanation: Independent judiciary organ of the government is required to protect the rights of the citizens.

questions.question: Which party has ruled Zimbabwe since its independence and who is its ruler?
questions.explanation: ZANU-PF, Robert Mugabe

questions.question: When did Zimbabwe attain independence and from whom?
questions.explanation: 1980, from White minority rule

questions.question: Democracy must be based on:

options.html: Choice from only the ruling party

questions.explanation: Free and fair election

questions.question: Which party always won elections in Mexico since its independence in 1930 until 2000?
questions.explanation: Institutional Revolutionary Party

questions.question: Why can the Chinese government not be called a democratic government even though elections are held there?

options.html: Some parts of China are not represented at all
Government is not accountable to the people
Government is always formed by the Communist Party

questions.explanation: Government is always formed by the Communist Party

questions.question: How many members are elected to the National People’s Congress from all over China?
One person one vote means:

options.html: One person is to be voted by all
A person can vote only once in his life
One person has one vote and each vote has one value

questions.explanation: It means that everyone in the country irrespective of the caste, colour, gender, creed and income earned is allowed to vote for his own representative.

questions.question: In which case was the real power with external powers and not with the locally elected representatives?

options.html: Both US in Iraq and USSR in Communist Poland

questions.explanation: Both US in Iraq and USSR in Communist Poland

questions.question: Who led a military coup in Pakistan in 1999?
questions.explanation: Pervez Musharraf

questions.question: The head of the government in Nepal is the
questions.explanation: In Nepal, the Government is headed by President.

questions.question: What kind of government is there in Myanmar?
questions.explanation: There is army rule in Myanmar.

questions.question: The word Democracy comes from the Greek word__________
questions.explanation: The word 'democracy' has its origins in the Greek language. It combines two shorter words: 'demos' meaning people and ‘kratia’ meaning power or rule. . So democracy is a rule by the people. This is the correct meaning.

questions.question: The word democracy has been derived from which word and of which language?
questions.explanation: The word 'Democracy' has been derived from the Greek words,'Demos' and 'Cratia'.Demos means 'people' and Cratia means 'power'.

questions.question: In which year Mexico became an independent country?
questions.explanation: Since its independence in 1930, Mexico has held elections every six years to elect its President. Before 1930 Mexico was under any military rule or the rule of any dictator. But it has been a democracy since 1930.

questions.question: Which one of the following is a country having one political party system?
questions.explanation: In China there is only one party system and party name is Chinese communist party.

questions.question: PRI stands for
questions.explanation: It is the full form of PRI. PRI  is the political party of Mexico.

questions.question: In which country free and fair elections are not held?
questions.explanation: Free and fair elections are not held in China and Mexico.

questions.question: In which country one person, one vote, and one value is not applied?
questions.explanation: In Fiji, the electoral system is such that the vote of an indigenous. Fiji has more value than that of an Indian-Fijian.

questions.question: Which one of the following is a demerit of democracy?

options.html: Democracy leads to delays in decision making

questions.explanation: This is the demerit of democracy, because usually in democracy decision -making takes much time.

questions.question: Who was the Prime Minister of Zimbabwe?
questions.explanation: Robert mugabe was the president of Zimbabwe since 1980 to 2017. He was the leader of ZANU-PF party.
