questions.question:
Which of the following is true with reference to Saudi Arabia ?
A. The country is ruled by a hereditary king.
B. It is a secular country.
C. The testimony of one man is considered equal to that of two women.
questions.explanation:
With refrence to Saudi Arabia:

A.The country is ruled by a hereditary king.
B.There is no freedom of religion.Every citizen is required to be Muslim. Non-Muslim residents can follow their religion in private,but not in public.
C.Women are subjected to many public restrictions. The testimony of one man is considered equal to that of two women.

Hence only A and C are true because there is no freedom of religion. It is not a secular country.

questions.question:
Which of the following is true with reference to the Guantanamo Bay ?

A. It is an area near Cuba controlled by American Navy.

B. It is an centre of International Protection of Human Rights.

options.html: Both A and B are true
A is false but B is true
A is true but B is false
Both A and B are false

questions.explanation:
VIOLATION OF CITIZENS’ RIGHTS BY THE USA:
1. About 600 people were secretly picked up by the US forces from all over the world and put in a prison in Guantanamo Bay, an area near Cuba controlled by America’s Navy.

2.AMNESTY INTERNATIONAL is an centre of International Protection of Human Rights.

Hence A is true but B is false

questions.question: The country is ruled by a hereditary king and the people have no role in electing or changing their ruler. With which of the following country the statement is associated?
questions.explanation: There was VIOLATION OF CITIZENS’ RIGHTS IN SAUDI ARABIA as the country is ruled by a hereditary king and the people have no role in electing or changing their rulers.

questions.question: An International organization of volunteers who campaign for human rights -
questions.explanation: Amnesty International is an international organisation of volunteers who campaign for human rights. This organisation brings out independent reports on the violation of human rights all over the world.

questions.question: In which year was the National Human Rights Commission set up in India?
questions.explanation: National Human Rights Commission is an independent commission set up by law in 1993. It focusses on helping the victims secure their human rights.

questions.question: What is meant by a secular state?

options.html: that which has to be neutral and impartial in dealing with all religions
that does not establish any one religion as the official religion
that does not confer any privilege or favour on any particular religion

questions.explanation: all of these

questions.question: Which one of the following is not a Fundamental Right?

options.html: Right to sacrifice animals under the Right to Freedom of Religion

questions.explanation: Right to sacrifice animals under the Right to Freedom of Religion

questions.question: Kosova was a province of which of the following countries?
questions.explanation: Kosovo was a province of Yugoslavia before its split.

questions.question: Amnesty International is an international organisation for
questions.explanation: Amnesty International is an international organisation of volunteers who campaign for human rights.

questions.question: Which of the following rights is available under the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Right to protect one's culture is one of the Fundamental Right i.e Cultural and Educational Rights.

questions.question: Which of the following freedoms is not available to an Indian citizen?

options.html: Freedom to oppose the central values of the Constitution
Freedom to start a movement to change the government
Freedom to participate in armed revolution

questions.explanation: Citizens have the freedom to hold meetings, processions, rallies, and demonstrations on any issue. But such meetings have to be peaceful. They should not lead to public disorder or breach of peace in society. Those who participate in these activities and meetings should not carry weapons with them.

questions.question: Which of the following is not an instance of an exercise of a fundamental right?

options.html: Workers from Bihar go to the Punjab to work on the farms
Men and women government employees get the same salary
Christian missions set up a chain of missionary schools
Parents' property is inherited by their children

questions.explanation:
a.Workers from Bihar go to the Punjab to work on the farms. : Right to Freedom.
b.Christian missions set up a chain of missionary schools. : Right to Freedom of Religion.
c.Men and women government employees get the same salary : Right to Equality.
However Parents’ property inherited by their children is not the exercise of Fundamental Right.The right to property is not a Fundamental Right but it is a constitutional right.

questions.question: The government shall not discriminate against any citizen on grounds of religion, caste, ethnicity, sex or place of birth. Mention the right with which the given statement is associated.
questions.explanation: As per the Right to equality. The government shall not discriminate against any citizen on grounds only of religion, race, caste, sex or place of birth. Every citizen shall have access to public places like shops, restaurants, hotels, and cinema halls. Similarly, there shall be no restriction with regard to the use of wells, tanks, bathing ghats, roads, playgrounds, and places of public resorts maintained by the government or dedicated to the use of the general public.

questions.question: Choose the right term: Selling and buying of men, women, or children for immoral purposes.
questions.explanation: The Constitution prohibits ‘traffic in human beings’. Traffic here means selling and buying of human beings, usually women, for immoral purposes.

questions.question: Under the PIL any citizen or group of citizens can approach ________ for the protection of public interest against a particular law or action of the government.
questions.explanation: Supreme court
